title: cleaning charge scam
text: Be aware before renting from Avis.  My first experience with Avis turned into a very disappointing one. I rented a vehicle for $195, but was later charged an additional $125 for “cleaning” over what amounted to minor dust and dirt. Charging such a significant fee for normal post-rental condition feels excessive and unreasonable.  After being a loyal Enterprise customer for over 5 years, I have never experienced anything like this. This experience has completely changed my opinion of Avis, and I will not be renting from them again.  If you’re considering renting from Avis, inspect the vehicle carefully, take photos before and after your rental, and be aware that you could face hefty cleaning charges. Rent at your own risk.

title: Rashad and Kumani at Avis on Powder Springs are terrible
text: I am a loyal Avis customer and rent vehicles frequently. Unfortunately, my experience with this location was incredibly frustrating and highlighted what appears to be a serious breakdown in both customer service and communication.  The day before my rental, I spoke with Kumani Owens and explained my situation. He instructed me to call the branch approximately one hour before my 5:00 PM pickup time to check on vehicle availability.  Wanting to be proactive, I actually called the branch the morning of my rental as well, knowing that I would also call back an hour before pickup as Kumani had instructed. I was trying to avoid any surprises and ensure that a vehicle would be available.  Following Kumani’s instructions, I called again approximately one hour before my scheduled pickup and spoke with Rashad, the manager. When I asked whether a vehicle would be available, his response was simply, “Probably not.” He did not say my reservation was canceled, nor did he definitively tell me that a vehicle would not be available.  Concerned by the uncertainty, I contacted Avis Customer Service. I spoke with two separate representatives who advised me that the branch should be attempting to locate a vehicle if inventory was limited rather than simply leaving a customer with a confirmed reservation without options.  Shortly afterward, I received the following text message directly from Avis:  “Your AVIS reservation number 1802*****has been confirmed.”  Like any reasonable customer, I interpreted that message to mean exactly what it said: my reservation had been confirmed.  Based on the information I had received, I went to the branch expecting that Avis would honor the reservation that had been confirmed through its own system. Instead, I was told there was no vehicle available.  What happened next was even more disappointing. Kumani acted as though I should have somehow known that the confirmation message was not valid because he believed Rashad had already told me there would not be a vehicle. That is simply not what happened. Rashad never gave me a definitive answer. He said “probably not,” and after that conversation I received an official Avis confirmation text stating that my reservation had been confirmed.  Rather than acknowledging the conflicting information I had been given or understanding why I reasonably believed a vehicle would be available, both employees were dismissive and unwilling to take ownership of the situation. There was no apology, no attempt to help locate a vehicle, and no effort to provide a meaningful solution.  A confirmed reservation should mean something. Customers should not have to guess whether a confirmation message from Avis is real, nor should they be expected to know that a branch has decided not to honor a reservation. At a minimum, customers deserve clear communication, professional treatment, and accountability when things go wrong.  This review is based not only on this incident but on multiple interactions with this branch. The recurring themes have been poor communication, lack of customer service, and an apparent indifference toward customer concerns. Avis deserves better representation than what I have consistently experienced at this location.  I hope the owner will take action and hire someone that can atleast get their poor customer service rating above a 4.0.

title: Worst reservation experience
text: I had an extremely disappointing and frustrating experience with Avis. I booked a car at one location expecting the reservation to be honored, but instead I was sent to three different locations. I had to pay for cabs out of my own pocket just to keep chasing a car that Avis had already confirmed but clearly did not have available.  What made the experience worse was the complete lack of coordination and responsibility. Avis did not properly verify inventory before confirming or redirecting me. Each location I was sent to was sold out, which wasted my time, money, and energy. This is completely unacceptable from a company that is supposed to provide reliable transportation.  A reservation should mean that a car is actually available, not that the customer is forced to run around the city from one location to another with no solution. The staff and system showed poor communication, poor inventory management, and no respect for the customer’s time or expense.  This was one of the worst rental car experiences I have ever had. Avis failed at the most basic part of the service: providing the car I booked. I would not recommend Avis to anyone unless they are prepared to waste time, spend extra money on transportation, and still end up without a vehicle.

title: Horrible customer service and policy
text: I was planning a beach vacation with my family and booked a rental car through the app under my name. Unfortunately, due to recent events and my military obligations, I’m no longer able to go on the trip with them. I contacted customer service to see if the reservation could simply be changed to my wife’s name. We share the same last name and the reservation is tied to the same credit card, so I assumed this would be a straightforward request. Instead, I was told it wasn’t possible. The first representative I spoke with was extremely unpleasant and completely unwilling to help. When I asked to speak with a supervisor, I was told, “I can, but they’re going to tell you the same thing.” When I finally did speak with a supervisor, that turned out to be exactly the case. The only option they offered was to cancel the reservation and rebook it under my wife’s name—which, because the trip is only two days away, would now cost an additional $500. There was no effort to accommodate the situation, no attempt to work with a loyal customer, and frankly no concern at all. What makes this especially frustrating is that I have been a very loyal Avis customer. Over the past six months alone, I’ve rented with them for more than 90 days. Despite that loyalty—and despite the fact that this request was simply to change the name to my spouse using the same payment method—I was met with rigid policies and some of the worst customer service I’ve experienced. Because of this experience, I will not be using Avis again. Between the inflexible policy and the absolutely terrible customer service from both the initial representative and the supervisor, this was incredibly disappointing. As someone who travels and rents cars frequently, I expected far better.

This wasn’t a last-minute cancellation for convenience — it was a forced situation due to unsafe travel conditions.  It’s frustrating that a company of this size can’t show basic consideration during an emergency. Situations like this make customers reconsider who they book with in the future. text: Leve express  checkout

Mixed experience.  I reserved a Chevy Suburban (or similar) months ahead of my 3 week family vacation for approx $1550.  Arrival and receipt of the vehicle was fine but there were immediately many issues: * vehicle was 2.5 years and 69k miles(!) old * my rental started on 9/14 and the car had just been registered on 9/8 in Florida and transported to Alabama. This makes you think where it came from …. * vehicle was very abused * vehicle was super dirty (peanuts, plastic trash, stains and mold). I spent 1.5h at a car wash before I put my family and kids in there for the trip * the car had an open safety (!) recall * tire repair kit was missing * wheels were out of balance causing severe vibrations above 60-65mph  I called the location on the first day but they didn’t help. I sent a long email with photos and got a call back many days later. I was offered to switch cars but I was already far away at the beach. Subsequently, I got a small discount on my rate but not much.  I never had Avis problems before but in the future, I will likely choose a different rental company.  For the same price, I could have gotten a BMW X7 from SIXT at ATL airport. I will do that next time and just drive the 2-3h to Alabama even though it’s an inconvenience.  The photos attached are all before my intense car wash.

text: This is more about Avis in general than this particular location. I booked a car for pickup at 11:30 pm. However, my flight was delayed such that I would not arrive until 2 am. I cancelled the reservation because, due to circumstances beyond my control, I would be halfway across the country and unable to pick up the car. I was charged a late cancellation fee of $150.  Which, fine. Terms and conditions and all that. However, a company that wishes to have repeat business will frequently not be so rigid about such things. That’s a lot of money for bad weather in DFW  essentially cancelling my whole trip. I complained to customer service. Customer service reminded me of terms and conditions and that was the end of the story.  Good job. You’ve got your $150 and I will never, ever, ever rent from Avis again.

text: TLDR: they will charge you for gas even if you turn the car in with more fuel than it had at pickup.  I'm beginning to think it's an industry standard, but they fraudulently charged me a fee for fuel. I suspect they charge a small amount extra on every rental, because most people won't fight a small charge. Mine was not much, just under $20, but absolutely impossible, and I have proof. (Previous similar fraudulent charges had me on my guard). I don't know whether it's rental car corporations that have set a policy of defrauding their customers, or if their employees are just stealing gas at customers' far higher expense.  According to the rental agreement, I was to be charged $8.99/gallon to bring the tank up to full. I was charged $17.75 for fuel, or just a hair under 2 gallons. But they could not have possibly fit another 2 gallons in that tank.  My first two photos were taken in the car pickup area. Fuel gauge just below the F line, fuel range estimated at 317 miles. Clearly, they do not fill the tank to maximum capacity if the needle is close to Full Third photo at drop off, 12 miles and 13 minutes after topping off fuel, fuel gauge still maxed out, and estimated fuel range of 448 miles. Final photo to prove distance and time from the last fuel up.  The car was getting a pretty consistent 34mp, so I burned about 1/3 gallon between buying gas and turning in the car, with about 3-4 more gallons of gas than it started with. The 2 gallons they charged me for would have required 60-70 miles depending on how hard I accelerated.
address: 1000 Glenn Hearn Blvd SW Ste 109B, Huntsville, AL 35824, USA

text: I have been unemployed for 2 years, burned out from the corporate career, and my wife is working 2 full time jobs. Now we owe $63,000 in taxes. A few days ago my wife and I celebrated out 16 year wedding anniversary on my birthday. We decided to rent a car to go visit a church in Nashville rather than add miles to our own car which we just had an engine replacement. We picked the mystery car to save some money and also added my wifes discount code to save more, and selected pay-now to save even more. The lady at the checkout counter noticed it was my birthday and selected a red mustang for me. It was a short 1 day rental for a last minute planned trip, but it made my day, it made our day and was the best rental I have ever had!! It was a joy to drive and I felt that there is still a chance for humanity in this evil filled world!! Thank you for making my birthday and wedding anniversary!! God bless you!! I will add that this is not the first time this location has saved my day!! They came through on several occasions as we were swapping out that engine in my wife's car. My truck is also broken right now so our car was our only daily driver. And there was 2 previous occasions when I needed to pick up a car for a Hertz reservation and the Hertz key-release requirements and scheduling were so strict that they would not release a key to me. So I had to do a walk up with Avis and they did get me setup so that my wife and I could go see the solar eclipse. I am a super fan of space stuff so getting to the center path of the eclipse was essential for me and Avis made it possible even last minute!! I love you guys!! Always there when we need them. The only problems I ever had was one time while driving down the freeway a wiper blade came off and flew away into heavy traffic. I tried to drive into the Birmingham Avis location and asked if they could just add a replacement wiper blade and they would not. This is a complete shame, and such a simple service should be common sense. Also if you get one of their EV - Electric Vehicle, do not bother with the ev charging station map app, its completely worthless. Just search for charging stations here on Google Maps. That is the way to go. Update: After all this good, Avis over charged us over $1000 for 11 days rental and then added another $317 for a dent that was already there on the car when we got it. My wifes car engine siezed up at the dealership after we just replaced the engine with a brand new remanufactured engine. We just needed a vehicle to get to work. She only makes $750 a month but we have to pay over $1000 for 11 days, and we have been trying to petition for even a partial refund for 3 weeks. So much for that good relationship. Avis is just as much of a scam as all the other rental companies!! They are all just out to make as much money as possible even if it means their customers end up homeless because no money left tp buy food or gas to get to work or mortgage payment!!
address: 1000 Glenn Hearn Blvd SW Ste 109B, Huntsville, AL 35824, USA

text: I just had the worst experience I have ever had in my life at a car rental agency at Avis in Dothan AL.  I had a reservation linked to my flight that somehow had the wrong dates to it.  When I arrived at desk to pick up my vehicle and they didn't have my reservation the employee figured out what had happened.  There was a prominent sign on the counter that said "We Have Cars" and she proceeded to get a car for me today.  I was appreciate of her help.  However, the manager from the side said we only have one vehicle "a van" ... I said a big van and she said "no a mini van"  I said okay, I don't need that but guess it will be fine.  There was no discussion of a price change... in fact there was never a discussion of a change other than date and car from the reservation I had made.  Unfortunately when the contract was printed for me to sign I assumed it was the same because there was no discussion of pricing at all.  While I was at counter waiting on the paperwork another customer returned a vehicle, I was not offered that vehicle either.  I didn't think twice because the paperwork was already being printed.   I was totally shocked when I pulled out the contract in the car to see it had more than doubled in price from my original reservation.  I drove on off thinking what a fool that I was and turned around in 40 minutes and brought the van back.  I said there was no way I was going to pay $600+ for a minivan I did not need for less than 4 days.   I admitted I had the wrong date on my reservation but pointed out they had never quoted me this high price... in any way. If they I would never have accepted it.  I had not driven 20 full miles but they charged me a full days rent, a full days insurance cost, a fully fuel service fee ($27 for 20 miles) and tons of other things.  I have used Avis and many other services before but I have never had this horrible of an experience.  It is  the manager... he knew I was desperate to make sure I had a way into town and to my class and I believe he took full advantage of it. He would not even come out to face me... I am contacting corporate with my complaint.  I did not mind paying a fare price for the time I had the vehicle.... but $140 for less than an hour is by no means fare, and I am guess somewhat illegal.  You can cancel reservations almost at any time and don't have to pay for the vehicle.  Why would I have to pay a whole day for 1 hour? I whish I had read the reviews before I chose this rental agency.  Not likely to do it again.
address: 800 Airport Dr, Dothan, AL 36303, USA

text: I have never had a worse experience with a car rental service in my life. I completely booked a car online, but upon arrival I was given an electric vehicle (EV) with no information about it at all. No one explained that it takes 10 hours for the fastest level of charging available. I’m literally stuck in a parking lot writing this review on my “weekend getaway” charging an EV until 6:00 am. Even if I was able to drive it back, according to the paperwork they ask you to sign without any explanation, it states if you don’t bring the vehicle back with at least a 10% charge or more you’ll be charged an additional $70. But that’s not all. Unlike other rental companies who will only charge by the hour past the scheduled drop off time, considering the customer’s departure flight is later than when they picked it up, they will require the vehicle is back exactly 24 hours after checking it out, or you’ll be charged an additional day, around $80+ or more. I highly recommend avoiding an EV on a road trip with a rental company because it seems like they want to scam you into paying more, and you’ll ultimately end up wasting your vacation in a random parking lot somewhere after spending hours finding the correct charging station.

text: I rented a Chevy Traverse (SUV) for my 10-days Alaskan trip (March 2025). Customer service was amazing. I missed my connection to ANC and called them to let them know I would be a day late. They answered the phone right away and changed my reservation to reflect my updated dates. The counter was easy to find at ANC, just follow the "Rental Cars" signs and they are towards the end. There was no wait at the counter and it only took a few minutes to take my information and get me my car. Just know that if you do not have your own insurance you will need to purchase it before they can release the car to you. They gave me a link to do it online on the spot, it was $20/day. Finding the car in the parking was easy, they give you the parking space number to look for within their section. The car was clean and AWD, just as described. There was a windshield scrapper in the trunk and a cable if you need to plug the car overnight. Returning the car was easy as well, follow the signs for "Car returns" and park in any available space in Alaska 4x4 section.  I drove to Fairbanks halfway through my trip and on the second day, I encountered some mechanical issue with the parking brake causing my rear wheels to stay locked. I called the ANC location and they picked up right away and were able to coordinate with the Fairbanks location to get me a tow truck and a replacement car, all of this within 1hr of me calling.  Alaska 4x4 was recommended by locals online and I can understand why. I'm usually an Enterprise customer but I wanted to make sure I had a car suited to the Alaskan roads and weather conditions. Alaska 4x4 delivered, and at a very affordable price too! I would 100% use their services again if I'm ever back in Alaska. Amazing customer service, clean and comfortable cars, and great prices!  Follow my Alaskan Trip on Instagram @soni.gallego
address: 8101 Dimond Hook Dr, Anchorage, AK 99507, USA

text: I rented two cars from this company, each for 11 days, back to back. Second one was the gravel approved, more pricy, but worth every penny for the possibilities it offered. I picked up the cars after hours, so they were staged at the valet parking in Anchorage. Wait for the shuttle at the airport was about 15 min. Process of retrieving the key was super easy. Both times I had Chevy Equinox, nearly brand new: first with 5k miles on and 2nd with 2k. Both cars were clean and in excellent condition.  No issues with either car throughout my rental period. Very roomy and comfy. Great on gas.  Returning the car at the valet was even easier than picking it up. Shuttle was ready to go.  The final receipt arrived the following day. Nothing was added to my bill.  I found the company to be reliable, honest, trustworthy, reachable, easy to deal with. Through my research I found them to be the most affordable for an suv, and one of very few companies allowing to take the car on gravel roads. Highly recommend them and if I’m ever back to AK they’ll be my go to.  Thank you Alaska Auto Rental. You helped to make my dream vacation a reality.

text: We rented a car from Yuma International Airport to Palm Springs International Airport (one way) on April 15, 2026. It was not in my vision for driving but it was a concern so I took a picture and I also called Budget to let them know about the windshield.  They assured me the windshield damage was notated in the notes for the car for damages.  I said ok and drove off.  When I returned the car in Palm Springs, there was attendant receiving the car.  I told him about the windshield and that I have notified Budget in Yuma before I drove off from terminal and was told damage had been notated.  The attendant also assured me of the same. Yesterday, May 6, 2026 I received a notice in the mail that I owed $669 for windshield replacement.  I am FURIOUS!!  Thank god I took pictures to cover myself!  I have only included the picture of the windshield, not the other damages.  My rental agreement shows I picked up the car at 12:58 pm and the pictures I took were time stamped 1:04 pm.  Also, the pictures show the surrounding area of being in the parking space at Yuma International Airport, before I even drove it.  I have sent my proof to Budget and am waiting for them to reverse the charges!! Always follow your gut instincts.  I had a very bad feeling and this is why.

text: This was the worst rental experience I have had in years.  I understand this is a licensed location and not a corporate Avis branch, but customers do not see that distinction when booking through Avis.com. I made a one-way reservation from Anchorage to Fairbanks using our corporate account, which includes contracted rates and vehicle protections.  At the counter, I was told our corporate rates and protections did not apply in Alaska and that our billed account could not be used. None of this was disclosed when the reservation was made. The only thing honored was the fact that it was a one-way rental.  We were then told the drive to Fairbanks was dangerous and that we “needed” studded tires. The SUV already had them installed, but they are treated as an add-on at $35 per day. We were also told our corporate protection coverage would not apply and that we needed to purchase LDW again. While technically optional, the implication was clear: if we were driving one way to Fairbanks, this was all “necessary.”  Fast forward to Fairbanks. It is -35 degrees and the vehicle will not start.  When I asked our hotel if they had a jump box, the receptionist asked if the car had been plugged in overnight. I told her it did not have a plug. She was shocked and said, “They didn’t give you a winterized vehicle?” The jump box did nothing. The car had to be towed — at my expense, since we declined the additional roadside coverage on a vehicle that should have been properly prepared for Alaska in the first place.  The Fairbanks Avis staff were surprised we had been sent north in a non-winterized vehicle and even asked if we had told the Anchorage counter we were driving to Fairbanks. We absolutely did — in fact, the Anchorage agent hand-wrote the Fairbanks phone number on our rental agreement.  The result: a stranded vehicle in extreme conditions, towing charges, and a missed excursion.  This felt less like customer service and more like aggressive upselling at the expense of safety and transparency.
address: 5000 W International Airport Rd, Anchorage, AK 99502, USA

text: Stranded in -40°C: Gross Negligence and Zero Support from Avis Alaska. I am writing this while currently stranded in Fairbanks because of the utter incompetence and lack of basic safety standards from Avis Anchorage and Avis Fairbanks. The Situation: We picked up our rental in Anchorage on Dec 24th. We explicitly informed the staff we were driving to Fairbanks. Despite knowing the extreme winter conditions, Avis provided a vehicle without an engine block heater. After the 6-hour drive on 26th dec, I called Avis again to check how can I plug in the engine block heater and if the car even has it, but Avis failed to even confirm that. The car naturally froze and refused to start in the -40°C temperatures. The Service Failure: Since Dec 27th, we have been stuck at our hotel, unable to get food or travel. We have called Avis Anchorage, Avis Fairbanks, Corporate Office, and Roadside Assistance dozens of times. • Avis Anchorage told us to talk to Fairbanks. • Avis Fairbanks refused to help because the car originated in Anchorage, telling us we were "on our own." • Roadside Assistance was a loop of transfers, at one point sending us to Avis Canada. The Cost: They refused to tow the car or offer a replacement. On Dec 29th, we had to pay out of pocket to tow the vehicle to a private garage just to try and get it running. We have been stuck in a hotel for 5+ days, spending hundreds on food delivery and extra lodging, and now face thousands in potential flight change fees because we cannot get back to Anchorage for our flight tomorrow. We are still stranded here in Fairbanks as even Garage folks have their own commitments till the New Years and we still haven’t got the car back yet. Avis abandoned us in life-threatening Arctic conditions. They provided equipment unfit for the climate and showed zero accountability for our safety. When we finally paid out of our own pocket to have the car towed on Dec 29th, the towing company and the mechanic were shocked. They couldn’t believe a rental agency would abandon clients in sub-zero temperatures or provide a vehicle so poorly equipped for the Alaskan interior. Being stuck in such a worst situation which wasn’t even our fault in the first place. Not even a single Avis person sympathised with our situation and offered any kind of help to us.  AVOID AVIS AT ALL COSTS IF TRAVELLING TO ALASKA. If you are traveling in Alaska, your life may literally depend on your rental agency—do not trust Avis at any cost.
address: 5000 W International Airport Rd, Anchorage, AK 99502, USA

text: This is my recent experience with Enterprise, and I wish it were the first bad one, but it is not.  Enterprise handed me a “standard” vehicle here in Alaska, while assuring me it had “winter” tires. If a cheap well used all season counts as a winter tire in their world, then my Yukon might as well be a Lamborghini Urus by that logic. Adjust your “standard” vehicle to seasonal change. Alaska is not a place where a front wheel drive with bargain tires qualifies as reasonable transportation for anyone, much less a family with kids.  Renting a small front wheel drive with low budget all season tires in Alaska, in the snow, to a family of five, is basically a shrug disguised as customer service. I would honestly feel better putting my family in a horse carriage and pulling it myself than relying on a company this inconsiderate and unprofessional. After this, I will be convincing my employer to avoid booking with Enterprise at all cost.  The pickup process was not any better. When the driver arrived, he yelled out “Who is waiting for Enterprise.” I responded along with another customer. We got in, and not even a minute into the ride I told him I needed their other location. He answered by saying he would not take me there and told me to change my reservation to his location. I eventually had to get my insurance representative involved just to get the vehicle I was supposed to get in the first place.  The vehicle itself was clean. The fuel level was not full, yet I am sure they will have no issue charging me as if it was.  Enterprise needs better customer service training. Employees who have no business working with customers should be kept away from customers entirely. This is my soapbox. Enterprise needs to do better.

text: What started out as a pleasant rental experience quickly turned into a frustrating and stressful ordeal.  Upon receiving my vehicle at the Phoenix airport, a tire pressure warning appeared before I had even exited the rental facility. I immediately brought this to the attention of the employee at the exit booth and asked whether the vehicle should be exchanged. I was advised that adding air to the tire should resolve the issue and that the alert would be cleared from the system.  After adding air, the warning temporarily disappeared. However, during our drive from Phoenix to Sedona, the tire pressure warning returned. We added air again and continued monitoring the situation. By the following day, it was obvious that something was seriously wrong. The vehicle's display showed the tire pressure reading at zero PSI, despite our repeated efforts to add air.  At approximately 10:00 a.m., I contacted Enterprise Roadside Assistance and was instructed to take the vehicle to the Cottonwood location. Before we could safely make that trip, we received another call advising us that the location had no available replacement vehicles and that we should instead take the vehicle to a tire shop for inspection.  We arrived at Big O Tires around 1:45 p.m., where the technicians quickly determined that the issue was not the tire at all. The vehicle had a cracked rim and was deemed unsafe to drive.  What followed was more than four hours of phone calls, transfers, conflicting information, and unnecessary delays. Tyler at the Cottonwood location was particularly unhelpful and provided some of the worst customer service I have experienced. At one point, he told me that if I wanted a replacement vehicle, I would need to Uber to the Cottonwood location. This was especially frustrating because he had previously stated that there were no vehicles available at that location.  I then contacted Roadside Assistance again and was told that a replacement vehicle was being sent. As time passed, it became clear that no vehicle was actually on the way. Eventually, I was connected with Brandon, who was professional, helpful, and ultimately got the situation moving in the right direction.  A tow truck was arranged for the disabled vehicle, and Enterprise then provided an Uber to transport us approximately 50 minutes to Flagstaff to pick up a replacement rental vehicle.  While I appreciate Brandon's efforts to resolve the situation, the overall experience was unacceptable. The vehicle should never have been rented with a cracked rim, especially after a warning light was already present before I left the rental facility. What should have been a simple vacation day turned into 4.5 hours of inconvenience, stress, and disruption.  This experience has left a very sour taste in my mouth and has significantly diminished my confidence in Enterprise's vehicle inspection process and customer service standards.
address: 1805 E Sky Harbor Cir S, Phoenix, AZ 85034, USA

text: We had a very disappointing and upsetting experience with Enterprise during our recent rental in Arizona.  About 60 minutes into our drive, the vehicle suddenly lost power and became inoperable, creating a serious safety concern. We were effectively stranded for over four hours and received no effective support during that time. Because of this, we missed an important university Preview Day event that was a major purpose of our trip.  We contacted roadside assistance and waited nearly two hours for a tow truck to arrive. However, the situation became even more concerning: •	The tow truck driver was unable to transport us to another rental location •	We were left at a gas station without assistance •	We were told that an Uber or taxi would be arranged, but no transportation was ever provided •	We ultimately had to find our own way （with help of gas grocery store ）to get transportation to Enterprise Presscott！and it is over 4 hrs we are still not getting a replaced car or any constructive assistance from Enterprise！  What made the situation even worse was how poorly it was handled afterward. With the help of a local gas station employee, we were finally able to make it to the Prescott Enterprise location shortly before closing and get another vehicle so we could continue with the rest of our plans. Without that local help, we might not even have made it there in time.  When we returned the car at the airport, we spoke with a manager expecting some level of accountability, a sincere apology, and a reasonable resolution. Instead, the response felt dismissive and perfunctory. At first, the compensation offered was only a very small amount. After we pushed back and explained again that we had been abandoned for over four hours, missed an important event, and dealt with a real safety issue, they finally agreed to waive 2/3 of the rental charges and reimburse us the transportation from gas station to Enterprise Prescott.  Most disappointing of all was the attitude. When we asked for a formal apology and a more reasonable response, we were met with comments such as, “Isn’t that partial waive enough?” and “Isn’t my verbal apology enough?” That response showed very little understanding of the seriousness of what happened.  This was not a minor inconvenience. It involved vehicle failure, safety concerns, lack of timely support, missed commitments, and dismissive customer service afterward. We also submitted a formal complaint and did not receive meaningful follow-up.  We are leaving this review because other customers deserve to know that when something goes seriously wrong, the response may be far below what you would reasonably expect.
address: 1805 E Sky Harbor Cir S, Phoenix, AZ 85034, USA

text: 5.29.26 11:45 pm - Will update as this progresses. We allowed them to select our vehicle because it was 'cheaper'. Assigned a Mercedes Benz 10 passenger van that only takes Premium Gas. They insisted we take photos of all damage and keep a record until our deposit is returned to us, 3-5 days after we drop it off. text: Upon waiting for my charges to be finalized, I found out I was charged an additional $348.35 on top of what I had paid at the time of scheduling my rental. Due to being a Type A person and having minimal income, I verified costs with the salesmen assisting me. He had noted that what I had paid at the time of scheduling the car was correct and how their would be a $300 hold and upon return of the vehicle it would be given back, made clear sense to me, feeling comfortable and confident of the exchange. Like I said, had a finalized posting of a withdrawal of $348.35. Upon outreaching to NU, they stated that amount was not the hold, but rather their basic insurance plan for the vehicle. As I also said earlier, I have very minimal income, so if there was something offered for additional money I would decline it. The woman I spoke to in this first interaction was very kind and understanding, stating she would send my agreement to the managers to review audio of the transaction. This weekend I got a call from Michelle who identified herself as a manager at NU and how the payment would stand as is due to the salesman telling me “3-4 times about the insurance plan”. I asked her if there is a way I can review this audio myself as this is did not ring true to me. I was told that 3 managers reviewed it and how they cannot send me the audio as other customers personal information is with it. If you have ever been to this location, you will know that they have almost a cubicle set up for transactions. As someone who has worked in hospitality and spoke kindly to these individuals, I find this to not be acceptable. I even told Michelle how if this was a small amount of money I would have never said anything, but where I paid more money for “basic insurance” then the actual vehicle itself, I found myself needing to say something. In addition to this frustration and nothing being resolved from it, upon picking up my car it was covered in scratches as well as blue paint which prompted me to take picture of the vehicle before leaving their lot and also smelt of cigarettes. Would not recommend their services to anyone and will not be a returning customer.

text: With 16 check in desks they only were using 2.  I waited in line for a little over 20 minutes. I made a reservation using credit card reward points. When I checked in I said I had a reservation, the Hertz employee was rushed due to the long line but didn’t say anything about my reservation. The person proceeded to have me initial a few places and gave me a car. A larger one than I rented. Upgrade!  That’s what I thought. Then the employee gave me a total. I told him I paid with credit card points and they said, “Oh this is for a security deposit.”  Tapped my card and walked out to the car. I saw a charged quite a bit more money than a security deposit on my credit card so I called Hertz to ask why.  Their AI phone tree is the worst. It was so frustrating to get to talk to a real person. When I did get in touch with a real person they said there was no record of me using credit card points on my reservation. In fact, there was a completely different reservation number the emailed reservation number from my credit card. We figured the Hertz employee who checked me in didn’t use the reservation I made, created a new reservation, told me the total was a “Security Deposit”, then charged me for an upgraded car!  I am furious. On the phone a case was opened and when I spoke with my credit card company they said they would not give me a refund until a resolution with Hertz gets resolved.  Be careful with this location. They will lie to you and charge you eventhough you have already paid through your previous reservation.  Update: After multiple calls with Hertz they admit no fault after their “investigation” and will not refund charges. Fortunately, American Express did refund my points so I wasn’t paying twice for one rental. I see Hertz doesn’t care for its customers. I hope they improve in the future or they will begin to lose business.

text: I’ve been a loyal Budget Rental Car fan for over 20+ years but times have changed seemingly since COVID. Here’s a quick summary of my last five rentals from Budget mostly in CO, and in AZ. Twice now in CO, I had to have my first car towed because the car kept shifting on its own out of gear. It’s a hard thing to be on vacation away from the airport and get a replacement car. Budget finally swapped the car after many phone calls to Customer No Service who puts you on extended call hoping you will hang up. My second car in CO was a complete screwup despite FastBreak service. When we pulled out, we were told we had the wrong car despite it being assigned to our FB parking spot. They gave us a new car but completely dirty both inside and out. Due to frustration I took it and found it had a bad wheel shimmy at 80mph. This last rental at Phoenix was a combination of the previous two. They gave me a FB rental in my spot but when I got to the gate they told me to take it back it was the wrong car again. We got a replacement car that had no heat but didn’t find this out until we travels from Sottsdale AZ to Sedona. We didn’t need heat until Sedona. We called Customer No Service again who put us in hold for over 30 mins. When the agent got on she pretended like she couldn’t hear, so I told her to get the stuff out of her ears and listen. She pouted and said we could take the car one hour plus north to Prescott for a swap. We told her that was unacceptable as we were on vacation. They refused to bring a new car so we drove an unsafe car who had issues keeping the windows defogged. Yes I accepted the risk, but we were on vacation 2+ hours from the Airport. We rented the car through Costco and called their Customer Service for an assist. They were helpful but could not resolve our issue. Budget finally offered us a $50 off coupon for our next rental. Like that will happen?  Budget knows how to take reservations, they just can’t provide clean nor reliable cars. We will no longer rent from Budget nor Avis. We will probably switch to Hertz to go with our Delta Skymiles. Bottom line is you get a budget vehicle when you rent from Budget. Dirty, unreliable cars backed by Customer No Service. The first photo is of the windshield after letting the car heat up for 15 mins. The second photo was two small holes in the ice that got us to a gas start where I defrosted the windshield. Oh yeah, no ice scrapper either.
address: 1805 E Sky Harbor Cir S, Phoenix, AZ 85034, USA

text: I wish I had read the reviews prior to renting. Do NOT rent here unless you like driving junk cars. Someone recommended them for renting to Rocky Point. Bad recommendation. When we first pulled onto the dirt lot, on the left side is older rental cars and on the right side is what appears to be a junkyard that they use to keep their rentals running or where they part them out when the finally die. The Uber driver says - are you SURE you want me to drop you off here?! It was sketchy looking at best. This should have been my red flag to RUN. The first two cars they asked me to choose from - one was in ROUGH condition - both bumpers held on the car with screws. Many dents.  It had the front bumper broke in half and they bondo’d it and spray painted it, getting overspray all over the grill.  So I went to the other unit, and it smelled like I was entering an ashtray.  I chose the rough one, but once we sat in it, it also wreaked of cigarette smoke. I went back inside and asked if they had any cars that didn’t smell of cigarette smoke. He said oh sorry, we must need a new machine for smoke removal. They suggested two more cars - one was 15-20 years old and I chose the other.  I had my wife give it the sniff test. It only smelled like rubber, so I guess that’s better than smoke. They hand me the key fob and it’s falling apart, splitting in two. They have all the door handle covers removed so you can lock and unlock the doors with the actual key since clearly the key fob is going to die at any moment. The left side of the front bumper is held on with screws. The right side of the fro t bumper was falling off. The left side of the rear bumper had a hole in it. The right front fender had been in an accident. Since the key fob is falling apart you have to try various positions to place the fob in the interior hoping praying and trying to get the car to start because the car doesn’t recognize the fob since it’s falling apart. The car was gross inside. Barely cleaned in some time. The steering wheel was worn out. Holes and cracks in the seats. The air pressure light was on. The car had a shake either in the front suspension or the tires. We picked the car up on Sat and on Sun it developed a shudder in the transmission. We were scheduled for Mexico on Monday. No way I am driving this car 4 hours to Mexico, around RP do 5 days, and back. When we returned the car early (Mon) - they said “no pro rata charges - you still have to pay for the week”.  We went back to the airport and rented a car, not a jalopy.

text: On 9/2025, I returned a rental car early in the morning. There were no visible employees, lane markers, or instructions indicating where returns should be made. Like many travelers do, I pulled to the front area where agents typically check in vehicles.  After I parked and began unloading my luggage, a vehicle sped toward me at a very high rate of speed, screeching to a stop just inches from my rental car. The employee driving the vehicle behaved aggressively and unprofessionally, yelling that I was not supposed to be there and demanding that I drive back around to the rear.  I attempted to calmly explain that there were no signs or staff visible when I entered. She continued yelling, claiming she had screamed at me to stop earlier, which I did not hear. I am handicapped, and being forced to move the car resulted in a much longer and more difficult walk back across to the airport. No apology was offered.  Later that day, manager Caleb contacted me, stating that the employee was very upset and crying about the incident. He asked what he could do to make the situation right. Instead of requesting a refund, I accepted his offer of two free rental days.  Since then, this has been nothing but a disappointment. Despite multiple follow-ups, no free days or points were ever applied, and each time I contacted Caleb I was given excuses and runaround. The commitment was never honored.  To make matters worse, the offer appeared to be conditional on leaving a 5-star review, which is highly unprofessional.  Based on this experience, I would strongly caution others about using National at this location. The employee conduct was unacceptable, and management failed to follow through on what was promised. I have retained text message proof of these conversations.
address: 1 Airport Rd, Little Rock, AR 72202, USA

text: I will never rent from this location again.  The manager was dishonest and changed the rental return time on my reservation. I originally reserved the vehicle from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., but when I signed the contract, the return time had been changed to 8:00 a.m. I was then charged a late fee for returning the vehicle after that time, even though it did not match the reservation I made.  When I questioned the discrepancy, I was told that vehicles could not be returned at 5:00 p.m. and had to be returned at 8:00 a.m. This explanation made little sense, especially since my original reservation reflected a different return time. As a result, I felt as though I was being set up to incur a late fee.  I explained that I desperately needed the vehicle for an additional day, but I was told I could not change the form of payment because of company policy. While I understand policies must be followed, I was frustrated by the lack of flexibility and the way the situation was handled. When I asked to speak with someone above the manager, no one would provide me with supervisor contact information.  After paying $646 for the rental, money was still deducted from my deposit, and I was treated rudely throughout the process. This location is a privately owned franchise, and in my experience, that made a significant difference in the level of customer service I received.  I actually liked the Toyota Camry I rented and had planned to continue doing business with Hertz for future trips. However, the customer service experience completely changed my mind. During a previous rental in January, I was also given a vehicle displaying an "Oil Change Required" warning.  I returned the vehicle at noon, yet I was still charged an additional $42 late fee. The constant changes in the explanation, lack of transparency, and unwillingness to address my concerns created unnecessary stress and inconvenience.  I also attempted to extend the rental for an additional day. I was first told I could not change the payment method, then told the rental would have to be closed out and restarted, requiring another $200 deposit. The entire process felt confusing and inconsistent.  What disappoints me most is that I wanted to continue renting from this company. Instead, I was left feeling that my concerns were ignored and that customer satisfaction was not a priority.  To make matters worse, I noticed that management routinely responds to customer complaints by directing people to Hertz's corporate website for assistance. However, Hertz customer service specifically informed me that they could not help because this location is a privately owned franchise. If corporate cannot assist franchise customers, posting the same generic link to every complaint does nothing to address the actual issue. It only gives the appearance of concern without providing any meaningful resolution.  The problems I experienced were not minor inconveniences. They involved changes to my reservation, additional fees, inconsistent explanations, and a lack of accountability. Businesses succeed when customers are treated with honesty, respect, and transparency. Unfortunately, that was not my experience at this location.  After being treated so poorly, I will not be returning, and I cannot recommend this location to others.
place_name: Hertz Car Rental - Jonesboro - Grant Hle
address: 1903 Grant Ave # A, Jonesboro, AR 72401, USA

text: I will NEVER use this service again. The poor customer service experience, complete lack of accountability, mistreatment and downright audacity from the employees & the negligence displayed towards our safety during the rental process was hands down the worst thing I have ever seen or experienced. First & foremost, the rental was not properly serviced. One of the tires had a leak and was losing pressure during the entire rental process & had to be aired up from flat multiple times a day when I was 4 hours away from the rental pick up spot & when I notified the rental location, I was informed by the employee who we processed the rental through that they were aware of the state of the vehicle and yet they chose not to disclose the issue nor made any attempt to fix it prior to pick up of the rental when we had booked the rental a MONTH IN ADVANCE. The employee informed me that this issue was mentioned by the previous renter of that same vehicle who had it about a week and yet with prior knowledge deemed it safe & appropriate to rent that vehicle to someone else. That same employee said quote "We thought it might have been because it was cold. So we did not think it needed to be serviced". By a stroke of luck, that same renter left their rental agreement in the vehicle so not only do I have info about that rental but also who the renter was and that alone stands as proof that the vehicle was not serviced in any respect because things like that would be removed & cleaned if it had. Then the supervisor whom I spoke to afterwards, who was incredibly disrespectful during this entire process, had the absolute nerve to tell me that because we had possession of the vehicle currently, this was our issue to resolve and we would be held accountable for any servicing needed for the rental and if we'd known the vehicle needed servicing that we should not have driven it off the lot. This boggles my mind. As a rental company, servicing of the vehicle prior to passing it to the customer is supposed to be standard, how in the hell is it my responsibility to fix an issue that you were aware of prior to me even finalizing the paperwork?! Not only was I out extra money to keep having to air the tire up every few hours but then finally even more for emergency repair when airing the tire was no longer sufficient. This negligence is insane and put myself ,my fiance, and the vehicle at risk and the responsibility falls squarely with your company. The supervisor specifically who is by far the worst representative of this company that I encountered during this whole process lied about the state of the vehicle, she took 0 accountability, shifted blame to us for everything, lied about time the rental needed to be returned, was rude to my fiance, filed a police report for a stolen vehicle after giving us an impossible deadline for return when we tried to extend the rental so we could GET THE TIRE SERVICED SO WE COULD SAFELY MAKE THE DRIVE BACK TO THE DROP OFF. My fiance was handling a great deal of the communication back & forth with Hertz to save me my sanity and the supervisor mentioned above was incredibly rude to him repeatedly. During the check in call that he did to inform the supervisor of the repair being made to the tire and updating on when the rental would be returned, she proceeded to tell him to not call back & she would not speak to him again. We kept in constant communication about the issues regarding the tire, the need for extension due to that, & the fact that this issue started while in a whole state away. We finally reached an agreement to extend the rental and pull the extra day out of the deposit but a stolen vehicle report was still put in despite our repeated efforts and open communication. The fact that an alternative payment method could not be used to extend the rental was very inconvenient and caused so much stress and unnecessary complications. PS If you rent a 2023 Nissan Altima from them just know it has a bum tire that was fixed with "fix a flat" by us and that will not last because I doubt they'll fix beyond that.

text: I am completely disappointed in my experience with Fox Rent a Car/ Europcar in Los Angeles. What was intended to be reliable transportation for a work training turned into a dangerous and distressing situation that placed my safety at risk.  I rented a vehicle for a professional training trip, expecting a safe and dependable car. On the morning of my training, the vehicle would not start. My sister and I attempted multiple times to turn it on without success.  Customer support insulted our intelligence about how to start and completely shut off a push-to-start car.  We were then advised that if we were able to start the vehicle later in the day, we could return it to the rental center for an exchange. As a direct result, I had to arrange alternative transportation via Lyft and arrived late to an important professional commitment.  That evening, we were able to get the car started. However, approximately five minutes into driving, the situation escalated quickly and became immediately life threatening when the vehicle completely shut off in the middle of an intersection.  The situation immediately became dangerous. The hazard lights did not function, and my driver-side door would not open. It was dark outside, and other drivers had no indication that the vehicle was disabled. My sister had to physically pull me out through the passenger side so we could exit safely. The vehicle then began rolling uncontrolled and struck a light pole in the intersection.  We waited over 40 minutes attempting to reach roadside assistance. Ultimately, LAPD arrived, worried about us and had to intervene and tow the vehicle because it was obstructing traffic and posed a public safety hazard. This experience was frightening and could have resulted in serious injury or worse.  Since this incident, the reimbursement process for the car rental has been unnecessarily difficult, along with the inconvenient and unexpected Lyft rides I was forced to take for the remainder of my work trip. In addition, I have been informed that records suggest I declined a replacement vehicle. I would like to clarify that after experiencing a vehicle that shut down in active traffic and demonstrated multiple mechanical failures, I did not feel safe accepting another vehicle from the same fleet. My decision was based solely on safety concerns, not refusal of service.  This vehicle was clearly mechanically unsafe and should not have been rented to any customer. As a business, it would be expected that if they actually cared about their customers, they would be accommodating throughout this entire process, not having me jump through several layers of communication to get this resolved.
place_name: Fox Rent A Car Los Angeles
address: 5251 W 98th St Suite 562, Los Angeles, CA 90045, USA

Drove back to the airport a few days later to charge and none of the high speed charging spots were in working order.  When I asked about this at the desk they said they were new and hadn't been turned on.  (eye roll) Clearly all of their staff doesn't know this.  Then come the end of my reservation they told me to just drop off the keys in the box since my flight was around 5am.  I get my bill a few days later and realize they charged me an extra day saying I'd completed the reservation at 6am istead of 5am.  Plus was charged over $200 for adding my husband to the car as an extra driver.  Even though that's illegal in California.  Eventually did just get it all sorted out but only after calling Avis general and sitting on the phone for 45 minutes.  Will definitely be looking for other options next time I visit.  Lost a potentially loyal customer who visits every couple months.

text: I had rented a vehicle in September, picked up from the valley and returned here at SFO. Everything went great until that point.  I had topped off gas right before pulling into the return area, so the fuel gauge was showing full (Drove maybe 2 miles after the gas station) and when the attendant gave me the receipt she didn't say anything to me. While walking away I noticed that the amount was higher than my prepaid rate with Costco. I went back to the booth, another attendant came and fired up the vehicle again and voila the gauge showed full tank, so she gave me a new receipt with correct charges. Keep in mind both attendants did a full walk through. I have OCD, so I always keep my own cars and any car that I drive in absolute pristine condition. It was a Cadillac XT4, so all screens were digital.  I thought this was the end of my nightmares. But 4 months later in Feb, I woke up one morning for my card charged around CAD 2,500 by Budget. My financial institution deemed it as a fraud and cancelled my card. I had not visited the states after September and had no recent transaction with Budget, so I disputed the charge with my bank. I then called Budget to figure how was my card charged and used. The lady then informed me  that I "Broke the radio" of my rental and hence was charged. Mind you, there was no communication, no information, and absolutely no rhyme or reason for me to be charged. I returned the vehicle in absolutely pristine condition and how could I even "Break a radio". This made no sense. But she was more like you gotta accept this charge and didn't budge despite my explaining.  I spoke with another agent after a few hours as CAD 2,500 is not a small amount. She investigated further and figured the charge was put on in error and decided to process a full refund. I was so happy that an hour and a half of being on the phone fixed it eventually. But, wait, nope it had not ended yet.  Due to my credit card being international, the financial institution uses a higher currency conversion rate when charging an amount and uses a lower conversion rate when processing a refund. So I got dinged for about CAD 130/USD 100. I have since spoken with agents who have been rude, refused to help, redirected me, promised me that I'll receive the refund, or worst tell me that it's a "me" issue, not theirs. I have sent emails, provided all proofs and done all that I could have.  Why should I bear the burden of a random fraudulent charge put by Budget's mistake? They'll do all they can to not own upto it and do the right by the customer. A few dollars of savings is not worth the hassle by choosing Budget. I travel a lot for work and personally too and never had any such issue ever. My first time renting with Budget through Costco and they tried their best to blame me for a damage done by another customer or their staff/crew.  Shame on this deceitful company and their unethical practices. If you Google you'll know that I wasn't the only one who had to go through this. It's a common practice for Budget to put random charges on customer credit cards on file.  Pictures attached were taken in Budget's lot in Santa Clara when I picked up the vehicle on Sep 18. There was a hairline crack in the screen which I told the attendant about (Zoom above the date in the first picture), but he mentioned that it was already registered in the system and nothing to worry about.  Budget Case#: 78895280

I then offered to step back and have my Japanese friend, who was with me, take over as the driver, but this was also rejected simply because I was part of the group. I was told this was company policy, yet there was no mention of such a rule on the website or during the booking process.  In the end, we had to urgently find another rental company, and fortunately we were still able to carry out our plans. When I got to Denver airport, I showed them my paper and was directed to a row of shiny new trucks. No farting about, no passwords or form filling. No haggling over extra insurance. The keys were on the dash. We loaded our stuff and left. Upon return, it was straight in, pull up. The man scanned a barcode on the dash board. No IDs required. A quick walk around and I was on the bus to the terminal. The trick is to use a Chase Sapphire, Visa CC. There is an annual fee but they cover the excess insurance on car rentals. So it pays for itself in one trip. Truck was a 2026 Dodge Ram. There was only one Ford and I offered to wrassle the other guy who got his mitts on it first. But my wife slapped me. So not my first choice but it is a sweet ride. Very quiet, plenty of giddy-up to climb the Rockies, and easy on gas. The dashboard has a screen like an I-Max which is a bit overwhelming and distracting, but I'm an ole fart so that's irrelevant. My Black Sheep was the perfect truck for skiing. I hauled everything and five people. It had a 4-WD feature which was a major selling point. I had looked at Turo but Enterprise was price competitive and a lot less arseing about. Will definitely use Enterprise again.

text: So as the organizer of our annual mens ski trip, I was looking for a pair of true four wheel/all wheel drive vehicles to take up into the mountains. We had eight men and a bunch of luggage including ski gear of course. After speaking with Nathan, I was a little apprehensive simply because it seemed really too good to be true! I went ahead and booked two Dodge Journey vehicles a few months prior to our trip. As the date approached, I checked in with Nathan to be sure he had received all of the necessary paperwork and he assured me he did. Upon arrival to the Denver Airport, my cell phone rang as I was walking through the concourse. It was Nathan telling me the cars were ready and waiting at the airport Valet. After a VERY SHORT WALK to the valet stand, we found our two beautiful and clean AWD vehicles ready and waiting. Our entire group was so happy that we did not have to get on a shuttle bus and stand in line at a traditional rental car place. We were on our way immediately  after loading up our gear and luggage. We literally experienced ZERO waiting to leave the Denver Airport! Consequently, we made it up into the mountains about an hour and a half earlier than we ever had in the past! While in the mountains, the AWD vehicles performed like a dream in the snow. We felt totally comfortable driving with the traction we were getting...Upon our return a week later, I received a text from Nathan stating that he might be able to meet us at the curbside checkin for our airline instead of us dropping the vehicles off at the valet stand. While in route to DIA from the mountains, we corresponded  via text to give Nathan our ETA. Upon our arrival at the airport, we were met by Nathan and his lovely wife Jennifer standing curbside right outside our airline of choice! We shook hands, unloaded our gear and walked into the airport. Done.....It was as easy as having a friend drop you off at the airport. Needless to say, this was the BEST RENTAL EXPERIENCE any of us eight middle aged men had ever had. And,collectivly, our group travels A LOT. The paperwork was a snap, the customer service was PHENOMENAL,and Nathan exhibited something corporate America has lost...KINDNESS...When in Denver, I will use Mile High Car Rental every time. It truly was RED CARPET TREATMENT!
place_name: Mile High Car Rental of Downtown Denver
address: 650 15th St, Denver, CO 80202, USA

text: We rented a tesla for a week and it was the worse experience with a rental car I have ever had. I would give them zero stars if that was an option. When we got it, the car was less than 20% and it was filthy. Other than that, the car was fine. We were told that the tesla had an adapter for use if we need at other non tesla chargers. When we got to the closest charger, which wasn't a tesla super charger, we were are about 4%. We went to get the adapter from where they told us it was and the tesla bag was empty. By this time, the car had gone onto its axillary power and we couldn't move the car. We called budget to see if they could either bring us the adapter that was SUPPOSED TO BE IN THE CAR or pay to tow it to the super charger a few miles down. They pretty much told us we were SOL. We had to call the local tow which cost us 300$ and when I called budget again after we finally got to a tesla charger, they said to provide them with the receipt and it would be reimbursed when we returned the car since the adapter that we were told was in the car and included with the rental was not there (which was noted on our file after I called). Obviously, if the adapter were in the car I wouldnt have had to get towed. I WAS TOWED OUT OF A CHARGING SPOT. We also prepaid for the tolls, but had to double pay the tolls because  it had an additional charge to the prepayment after returning. text: If zero stars were an option, I would select it.  I am a multi-year Fastbreak customer and have rented vehicles across the country without issue. This particular experience was entirely unacceptable and far below any reasonable standard of service.  This was a 10-day, cross-country rental. The vehicle was returned in proper condition, having been maintained carefully throughout the trip. Upon return, I was accused of a “strong marijuana odor”—a claim that is categorically false. We do not smoke in any form. There was no odor, no evidence, and no objective basis for that assertion.  Rather than approaching the situation professionally, staff immediately treated the accusation as fact and attempted to impose a smoking fee without substantiation. The interaction was not handled as an inquiry, but as a presumption of wrongdoing. Quite embarrassing in front of other customers.  In an effort to resolve the situation, we independently cleaned the interior of the vehicle and returned it for reinspection. At that point, we were told we “passed” provided the odor did not return—again, referencing a condition that did not exist to begin with. This inconsistency only further calls into question the legitimacy of the original claim.  To be clear: there was no marijuana, nicotine, or smoke of any kind present in the vehicle at any point during the rental period. The allegation of a “strong odor” was completely unfounded.  Budget corporate ultimately reviewed the matter and removed the $250 cleaning fee. While that outcome was appropriate, it does not excuse the initial conduct, the lack of due diligence, or the manner in which this situation was handled at the local level.  A review of other customer experiences suggests a concerning pattern involving disputed cleaning fees (to include the EXACT accusations of several other customers re: marijuana odors) unsupported claims, and poor customer treatment. That pattern warrants serious consideration.  Going forward, I will choose to rent from airport locations where there is greater oversight and accountability, even at a higher cost.  I would strongly advise others to proceed with caution at this location.
address: 384 Garden of the Gods Rd, Colorado Springs, CO 80907, USA

text: My partner and I rented a car from this location back in February of this year. We had no issue during the rental process, and everything went smoothly. However, last week he was sent notification that he was being charged $150 for a parking ticket that was allegedly received during the time of the rental. We never received any kind of notification of this in the months since this trip. Upon investigating further, he discovered that the ticket was issued in error as we had paid to park in the lot and had been dismissed on the city's website. He found not only the receipt from where parking was paid for, but also took a screenshot of the dismissal on the city website. The agent he spoke with at Sixt was quite unhelpful and shared that, even though this was likely their error, he would still need to provide evidence that this was incorrect. That is no problem at all. The problem is that they stated that in 10 days his card would be charged if this was not resolved. When he said he was emailing the proof over, he was told that no one would be back with him for at least 5-7 business days and that was no guarantee that his card still wouldn't be charged after 10 days. How are you supposed to resolve this if we were only given 10 days to respond even though it had been MONTHS since the rental and this is the first we've heard of it? Not to mention that we have evidence that this was an error not due to any fault of ours. This seems like very shady business practices because they make it so difficult to resolve that I'm sure most people just give up and the company then pockets the $150 charge. DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY!

text: 1 star — Wasted two hours, lost my reservation, still waiting on my refund.  I arrived on time and waited over an hour in the lobby before they brought my car out. While loading my things, staff told me I couldn’t leave my personal vehicle on their lot. I’d been allowed to on a previous rental here, and nothing in my reservation warned me otherwise. The only nearby option was a 2-hour street parking spot with tow-away enforcement, which was useless for a multi-day rental. I offered to sign a liability release waiver. They refused. They offered no alternative lot, no other Enterprise location, no solution at all. Just “move it or cancel.”  I had no choice but to cancel. The whole reason I rented was that my own car isn’t reliable enough for the 4.5 hour drive to see my fiancée, and now I couldn’t make the trip at all. The worst part is I still haven’t been refunded. They voided the $300 deposit but the original booking charge has not been returned. text: If I could leave 0 stars I would.  I’ve had the worst experience with Avis.  I never leave reviews but I feel the need to warn others about them so the same thing doesn’t happen to them.  Me and my son flew into Bradley international airport for a college visit at UConn in early April.  We were there for less then 2 days but needed a car so ended up renting a car from Avis.  We took good care of it, didn’t eat or drink anything in it, filled it up with fuel, and returned it spotless, looking the exact same as when we got it.  A couple weeks later I get a letter in the mail from Avis saying they’re charging me $450 for an extra cleaning fee.  I called them and spoke to a customer service rep and asked what the fee was for, and he said for extra cleaning.  I said how, we left it spotless.  He put me on hold for about 5 minutes and came back and said the charges were being removed.  Great!  Or so I thought.  A week later I start receiving calls and getting letters from a collection agency.  Avis had sent it to collections!!! Are you kidding me?!  Now I’m calling collections telling them my story, they’re telling me I need to call Avis.  I call Avis back and the gal said all I would need to do was email Avis and get pictures for proof, so that way they would close the case if there was no proof.  Perfect I though.  They can’t prove it because I know I didn’t do anything wrong.  I email them, and all they say is that the charges can’t be removed.  Can’t provide pictures or even tell me what was wrong with the car.  Part of me wants to pay the $450 just to be done with it and stop wasting time dealing with a terrible company trying to scam me, but I know that’s not the right thing to do. So here I am writing this to warn anyone that’s thinking about using them, Don’t!!!  They might try to scam you, like they are to me!  I am telling everyone that I know about this to help protect them as I don’t want this to happen to others.  This isn’t right and they should be ashamed.
address: 180 Schoephoester Rd, Windsor Locks, CT 06096, USA

text: I had a very disappointing experience with Avis at Bradley International Airport. Because of these issues, I immediately returned the vehicle and informed the staff that I no longer wished to continue the rental. I wasted a whole day yesterday trying to rent something from this enterprise in the same way as i rented every other time i've rented from enterprise in the past up until last week. Yesterday was a disaster, I thought i was prepared for everything today but I guess i was wrong. Being a plus member i figured having a history using them would have helped also. Apparently now you have to have the actual paperwork for your insurance to rent a car now. Not what they have saved on your account or what you can pull up on your phone. You'd think they'd tell you that when you reserve the car online. Why would I bring that with me? What if it was an emergency? Well That's  what the "manager" said at least. personally i think it was malicious. But i can't prove it. i just know. They also couldn't do rides this week. I got a ride last week though. Maybe it was a different manager. I know the guy last week was so much more efficient and there wasn't a line of people waiting. I won't be back to this one though . I'll go to Milford or Dover. Their space is cleaner, people are nicer and the property doesn't look like an old dirty used car lot. this last part is an update. Someone explained to me that some managers try to "bully" you into using their insurance. Which now actually helps everything make sense. Sense to what happened not sense to customer service. And it's funny that they asked me to contact them about my concerns in a message below but of course they're not open.

text: Completely Unacceptable Experience This is, without question, the worst car rental location I have ever encountered in nearly 20 years of living in Delaware.  The Newark, DE Avis facility is in deplorable condition—run down, poorly maintained, and resembling an old warehouse rather than a customer-facing business. More concerning, every vehicle I was offered reeked of marijuana and smoke. I was initially given a Toyota Corolla. Upon immediately reporting the strong weed/smoke odor, I was reluctantly switched to a Toyota Camry—only to find it smelled just as bad. I was then told no other vehicles were available unless I paid to “upgrade” to an SUV. This is unacceptable, especially when the issue is basic cleanliness and safety, not personal preference.  Neither vehicle appeared to be properly cleaned—inside or out. The lack of sanitation alone is alarming. To make matters worse, the two employees I dealt with were disturbingly casual and dismissive. There was no apology, no ownership, and no attempt to resolve the situation professionally. The complete absence of customer care and accountability was shocking. The car had a huge scratch too and I had to go inside to report it, the customer service personnel didn't even go out with me to walk around like they usually do to check for any dents or scratches. Because I have a 5:00 a.m. airport drive tomorrow, I had no choice but to take the Camry. I am now personally cleaning the vehicle and using air fresheners just to make it tolerable to drive—something no customer should ever have to do.  I have formally reported this incident to Avis Corporate Customer Service and have been issued a case ID. This location reflects extremely poorly on the Avis brand. I strongly advise anyone needing a rental to avoid this Newark, DE location entirely. This is service at its absolute worst.
address: 915 S Chapel St, Newark, DE 19713, USA

text: My problem is not so much with the Dover location as it is with the company. I have rented numerous vehicles over the past two years, and have been completely pleased each time. On Friday, March 13th of this month I rented a vehicle to attend my granddaughter's gymnastics competition on a rare occasion to do so in Philadelphia. The car was not completely cleaned inside ( which I cannot complain about because I arrived a little early). Then I noticed something hanging beneath the vehicle. I got down on all fours and noticed it was a trash bag stuck to the underside of the car, and thought nothing else of it. The next morning when I entered the vehicle to travel to Philadelphia, I immediately heard something dragging underneath. I exited the vehicle and saw that trash bag had been dislodged ani revealed a part of the car was dragging underneath. Not wanting to damage the car, I immediately stopped, pulled back into my driveway and called Avis. This was just after 9:00a.m. March 14th. The next seventy-five to ninty minutes were like I was stuck in a movie getting accidentally disconnected, getting conflicting information, getting talked over while trying to explain the problem. I requested a tow truck to retrieve the vehicle (which was already paid for at the time it was reserved). text: Absolutely terrible experience with Budget Car Rental at Orlando International Airport. The agent was pushy and aggressive about upselling extended insurance—even after we clearly explained we already had full coverage through our provider at home. Not surprisingly, when we got to the garage, we were assigned a Hyundai Kona—a compact SUV that barely fit two suitcases and is absolutely NOT what we paid for. To make matters even more concerning, when we asked them to properly document existing damage on the vehicle, they refused and told us it “wasn’t considered damage.” We explained we had a prior experience with them where damage wasn’t recorded and we were later sent to collections over it—something we only resolved because we had timestamped photos. In the end, we did get a Chevrolet Equinox—which is what we had paid for in the first place—but only after wasting 45 minutes dealing with rude, dishonest, and manipulative staff.
