text: I was extremely disappointed with this rental car company because they charged multiple extra fees that were never clearly explained at the time of booking. What initially seemed like a reasonable price quickly became much higher due to unexpected and hidden charges added at pickup and even after returning the vehicle. They told us that we needed to return the car within 24 hours. We picked up the vehicle late at 11:38 p.m. and returned it at 7:00 p.m. the next day, which was clearly within a 24-hour period. However, they still added a $15 late fee. When I later reviewed the paperwork more carefully, I noticed the form mentioned something about a 12-hour policy, which was never properly explained to us at the counter. Because of that, they charged another late fee without clear justification and simply told us we were “lucky” when we questioned it. In addition, we specifically asked them to remove the loss damage waiver, but they failed to take it off and still charged us for it. We were very tired at the time and did not fully review the paperwork before signing, so that part was our mistake. However, the staff should still be more professional, transparent, and clear about the charges being added. In total, we were charged about $60 more than we originally expected. The lack of transparency and honesty made the entire experience frustrating and stressful. I would strongly advise others to carefully review all terms and conditions before booking, and personally, I would not recommend using this rental car company again due to the numerous hidden fees and poor customer service.

text: 3.5 stars  Overall, my experience was good, but Hertz could significantly improve the customer experience with better signage and clearer communication from staff  When I arrived, there were signs everywhere directly guests that were a part of their elite membership programs, but I didn't see any clear signage or direction for regular customers. As a result, I walked into the first booth I came across and waited to be helped, unsure if I was even in the right place.  The representative who assisted me was friendly and seemed to recognize that I was in a bit of a hurry, and I appreciated his efforts to move the process along. However, he didn't do a great job explaining what was happening. While I was trying to provide my reservation information, he asked me to step out of the booth and check whether my name was on "the board." I wasn't sure which board he was referring to or why I needed to check it, and no explanation was offered.  Despite the somewhat confusing check-in process, the experience improved considerably from there. I was pleasantly surprised to learn that I could choose my own vehicle from a designated section rather than having one assigned to me. As a customer, I value my autonomy and being able to make the best decision for myself, and I appreciated the opportunity to select a vehicle that suited my preferences. It was a small detail, but one that made a meaningful difference in my overall experience.  I also appreciated that the vehicle was very clean and smelled quite nice when I entered it.  With better signage for non-elite customers and a little more clarity about what is expected during the check-in process, this could easily be a five-star experience. The service itself was acceptable and the vehicle condition was good but it was the lack of direction and communication that created unnecessary confusion.
place_name: Hertz Car Rental - Orlando - Orlando International Airport (MCO)
address: 1 Airport Blvd, Orlando, FL 32812, USA

What really set this experience apart, however, was the incredible friendliness of the staff on-site. Despite managing a high volume of travelers, the team at the counter was smiling, welcoming, and genuinely attentive. The representative who assisted me was an absolute delight—efficiently pulling up my digital reservation, walking me through the details with zero high-pressure sales tactics, and sending me off with genuine southern hospitality. The vehicle itself was spotless, smelled fresh, and ran beautifully. Returning the car was just as smooth, with the attendants making drop-off a total breeze. Enterprise at ATL takes the stress right out of travel. Between their effortless digital check-in and their gold-standard customer service, they have officially won me over as a loyal customer. Highly recommended!

text: The only reason I am giving a 2 star review is because of the nice young women that helped me out. text: The pickup experience at Sixt ATL was actually smooth. The new key process took very little time. Unfortunately, that’s where the positive experience ended. Second, the exterior of the vehicle was not cleaned and it was visibly dirty, which immediately raised concerns about overall inspection standards.  More importantly, once driving, there were serious mechanical and quality issues. At speeds over 40 mph, the car shook significantly, which made the drive uncomfortable and concerning from a safety standpoint. On top of that, the center rearview mirror was coming apart, clearly showing wear or damage that should have been caught before the vehicle was released. Clearly this car was involved in a major collision and I’m just sharing because, Sixt will be real quick to charge you so document the condition if your rent with them.  In short: great pickup process, but the vehicle condition did not match the level of service I’ll usually would expect at this price point. On the other hand, National Rent-A- Car executes flawlessly every time so maybe they should poach from someone over there. text: I had originally rented a premium Dodge Durango (or similar) and received a basic Dodge Durango (cloth seats, no sunroof, no frills).  All options in the lot were basic trims with cloth seats and no sunroof.  But it was clean and comfortable so we went with it and it was a great vehicle for our first 6 days.  After 6 days we decided we wanted to upgrade to something larger as the luggage space just wasn’t enough for an excursion we were going on with 7 passengers on board.  I called several branches and no one had a larger vehicle, and they said we would have to just go back to the airport and hope they have something since you can’t get them on the phone (1 hour round trip).  Luckily there was a beautiful Jeep Wagoneer L which we scooped up.  There was only one other option (Chevy Suburban) which had a weird smell so we went with the Jeep.  My only initial disappointment was that there was no sunroof again, and looks like that is just standard across all vehicles I could see.  The garage was dark and the interior was black so we couldn’t really see the interior until we got home.  Once we got to our destination, we noticed the interior had maybe received a quick vacuum, but the previous drivers had sunscreen stains on the seats, food smeared all over the seats, beach sand in many areas, etc. We didn’t have any time to go back and swap again and our youngest has severe food allergies (peanut, soy, etc), so my wife spent the next 30 minutes cleaning out and wiping down the interior of the vehicle.  We always wipe down vehicles anyways but it’s usually a 5 minute job.  I went in the app to look at options for sending feedback, and it then noticed that I had been back charged as if I had this vehicle since the beginning of our rental (extra $200 when it should have been $80).  I sent all of this feedback through both the app and via email, with pictures (and followed up via email) but have yet to hear from Enterprise.  First message March 2nd, second on March 5th, then shared again when I dropped off the vehicle.  When I returned the vehicle, I shared this feedback and the billing was adjusted for the overcharge, but nothing offered for the condition of the vehicle.  To be fair she asked what she can do and I mentioned that I already sent a message to corporate and would await their response since we were running to our flight.  She said she would adjust the billing and she did right away which was great, and I’m grateful I didn’t have to wait for corporate.  Please keep in mind this is coming from someone who used to work for Enterprise 20+ years ago, at an airport.  I know the standard we had then and I would have wanted this feedback.  I hope to receive a call or have a response that you received the feedback this time.
address: 300 Rodgers Blvd, Honolulu, HI 96819, USA

text: I was disappointed with my rental experience.  First, the pickup was problematic. After loading my children, I noticed a broken panel in the back. I later found out that some vans were in even worse condition than mine, with scuff marks and scratches all over.  The worst part was the drop-off. The person who came to the car scanned the sticker on the windshield and told me to remove my belongings without saying hello or anything.  I had to track down another employee to discuss the panel issue and avoid getting charged.  While I was talking to this person, another employee decided to open the back door, which caused my stroller to fall and break the cup holder.  I now have to get this fixed, and there was no apology from either employee.  Overall, I would have expected Enterprise to have better training for its employees. text: Overall, a decent rental car for the price we got compared to some of the national brands. We stayed at the Hilton Hawaiian Village and wanted a convertible. Island Car Rental had one for a very reasonable rate compared to the national brands so we took a chance.  The booking process was a bit long but nothing out of the ordinary. Once you make the reservation, you get several emails with instructions on how to pick up the vehicle. I will say this part was a bit confusing. We followed the video as instructed but then found out the garage level in the video is different from the email, follow the email instruction. I do wish there were signage of where the rental car was located.  This part I don't know if I missed in the email or not, but be prepared to come with proof of insurance. This includes credit card car rental insurance. They require a letter from the credit card issuer to be able to pick up the vehicle. I ended up just using our personal car insurance to speed up the process.  I also wish we had walked the vehicle before we left. The attendant was very quick to get us out of the garage which we appreciated. The issue I had was later in the day, I noticed some damages to the vehicle that we weren't sure if it was documented.  Lastly, either it was coincidental or I didn't get the email response, but we requested the Blue Mustang because it's my fiance's favorite color, and I was delighted to get exactly what we asked for.  This was my first time renting outside of my comfort zone, we typically always rent from National. I will say, it was a good experience and the rates here were comparable with my company corporate rates at the big brands. I recommend renting here, just come prepared with your proof of insurance and walk the vehicle to point out any damages.
address: 1765 Ala Moana Blvd, Honolulu, HI 96815, USA

text: We had a somewhat mixed experience using Car Rentals of Hawaii  Positives: The price is great We had no issues with the car. It had 28,000 miles on it and showed a little wear-and-tear but no complaints  Negatives: Communication is the big issue here.  We had a reservation to pick up the car a little after noon, but with some delays related to the flight and getting an Uber, we get to the office at 1pm to find there is no sign of anyone and spend 10min calling the two numbers they have on the wall. Someone finally calls us to let us know that the car we were supposed to get got broken into the night before.  They tell us they can maybe get a car for us or they can offer us a refund. We opt for the car and they were able to get us one within a couple hours.  We understand that things can happen outside of their control, but the issue I have is that at no point either before our reservation time or in the hour it took us to get to the office did they reach out to us to let us know there was a problem. Instead we arrived at a locked, empty office repeatedly calling and then spent two hours waiting around in the heat without access to a bathroom.  Another minor comment is related to the drop off at the end of the rental period. We were instructed to leave the car in the lot, put the keys in the mail slot, and send an email indicating we returned it. I would've appreciated some kind of acknowledgement from the business that they received the email/car/etc but we just never heard from them again.

text: I recommend taking a few extra minutes to inspect any rental vehicle before leaving the lot, especially checking the safety inspection date and basic fluid levels.  I recently rented a 2022 Chrysler Pacifica for a week. After getting the vehicle home, I noticed the safety inspection had expired, the maintenance reminder indicated the oil change was overdue, and the second-row floor storage compartments could have used a quick cleaning.  When the main customer service line was unable to provide timely assistance, I returned to exchange the vehicle rather than risk potential issues while driving. To their credit, the staff quickly provided a replacement vehicle. While waiting, I checked several other minivans nearby and noticed multiple vehicles also appeared to have expired safety inspections. My concern wasn’t to be critical, but to hopefully prevent another customer from unknowingly driving away with the same issue.  The replacement vehicle had a valid safety inspection, but I noticed the engine oil, coolant, and right rear tire pressure were low. I asked if the fluids and tire pressure could be checked. The staff promptly brought the vehicle to maintenance, though afterward it appeared the coolant level still had not been addressed. At that point, the team again offered to exchange the vehicle, but I decided to keep it since the major safety and registration concerns had been resolved.  I do think the maintenance oversight team could improve its inspection process. Basic items like current safety inspections, fluid levels, and tire pressure should ideally be addressed before vehicles are rented out. Attention to these details helps prevent breakdowns and gives customers more confidence in the condition of the fleet.  That said, I want to recognize the customer-facing staff for being respectful, patient, and willing to help throughout the process. In particular, the on-duty manager, Peter, was professional, attentive, and genuinely listened to my concerns. He handled the situation very well, and I appreciated his attitude and effort.  For other customers: regardless of which rental company you use, it’s always worth doing a quick inspection and documenting any issues before leaving the lot. A few extra minutes upfront can save time and frustration later.

title: Horrible company
text: I had an absolutely terrible experience with Budget at MCO Airport, and I would strongly advise others to avoid this location.  I was charged $150 for a rental car that I never even picked up. My flight with United Airlines was delayed, and during that same timeframe Budget was experiencing internet/system maintenance issues. Because of this, I was unable to cancel or modify my reservation. Despite these circumstances being completely out of my control, I was still charged—completely unreasonable.  To make matters worse, the staff at the MCO counter were rude, dismissive, and unwilling to help. There was no attempt to understand the situation or provide any level of customer service. I was treated like an inconvenience rather than a paying customer.  This entire experience was frustrating, unprofessional, and honestly felt predatory. Charging customers under these conditions while providing such poor service is unacceptable.  I will never use Budget again, and I will be actively discouraging my family and friends from doing so as well. There are far better rental companies that actually value their customers.  Avoid this location if you care about fair treatment and basic professionalism.

title: Unable to use the app when I need to
text: I rarely write app reviews but this was such an egregious offense that I felt I must leave my feedback for the developers.  I downloaded this Budget app before my international flight. I logged in before leaving and all of my rental information was there, ready for me when I arrived at my destination.   After landing in the foreign country and collecting my bags, I opened the Budget app as I walked to the rental counter. Instead of displaying my rental details, the Budget app presented me with a blocking modal screen that informed me that an app update was available. However, there was no way to dismiss this blocking message. The only option available was to download the updated app from the App Store, which I could not do doing over my cellular connection on international roaming.   It is absolutely absurd to block the use of the app until an update can be downloaded. I wasn’t even using a very old version of it anyway, I had downloaded this one 2 days prior.   Developers: provide a way to allow use of the Budget app even if there is an update available. As it stands now the app is completely useless when this situation arises.

title: I’m refundable early vehicle return
text: On February 19, I rented a vehicle through Budget with a scheduled return date of March 1. The total charge was $493.00 and some cents. However, after months of vehicle issues, my personal car was unexpectedly repaired sooner than anticipated, and I returned the rental on February 21—after approximately 2½ days, not even three full days.  At the time of return, I was explicitly told that the difference would be refunded. I returned the vehicle in proper condition and well within policy expectations. Despite this, I am now being informed that no refund will be issued and that Budget intends to keep the entire amount.  This is deeply disappointing and feels dishonest, especially given the assurances made at the time of return and my history as a frequent and responsible renter. Retaining the full amount for a rental that was returned early is not reasonable, nor does it reflect the level of service I would expect from you all.  I am deeply deeply dissatisfied!

To correct the drop off location.  Because I refused to pay 100’s for an error I didn’t make I was forced to hurriedly return the car which caused me to almost miss my flight. Just to cancel.  I spoke with 3 separate agents  Not one of them could help, take responsibility or offer a reasonable solution. The lack of accountability and basic customer service is shocking!  Bottom line  Budget/Avis made a critical error  Refused to fix it without charging hundreds of dollars, put my travel plans at risk, and still found a way to charge me anyway .  My advice to others  DONT PREPAY  TRIPLE CHECK ALL THE DETAILS YOURSELF.  If anything goes wrong, expect fees instead of help. text: I wanted to change my rental by extending it by one day. I went on the app which I use to book frequently.   There is no place under your current rental where it says or allows you to change the reservation.   After going through the automated route of trying to extend, that didn’t work. (Mind you I’m only trying to extend by one day. Drop off the next day at the same time and same location). I then had to go through ALL of the information AGAIN with a live agent that made things even more frustrating. Regardless, my total rental (6 days) was $380. For adding exactly 24 more hours, it was over $100.  And although not an enormous amount of money, that is a ~35% increase compared to the entire rental. Ridiculous. - rethinking Budget in the future now. This is for being a loyal customer.

title: Dangerous car!
text: We rented a car at London Heathrow on 17 August ‘25 and they sent us on our way. We drove to some very remote places in both England and Scotland, while in Edinburgh at 9pm while preparing to leave for Glasgow the car wouldn’t start and budget was no help as all they suggested was for me to tow it to the airport (office closed) to get another car. I called AA who asked if this was the same battery problem that was reported on the 17th as the names didn’t match we surmised he reported the problem and nothing was done and they rented an unsafe car to me.  AA came and all they could do was give me a jump start which I did then drove to Glasgow without shutting it off. The budget office luckily was a 10 min walk from my hotel but they wouldn’t give me another car even though there were people renting them, but told me to tow it to the airport. No pickup and swap, no customer service, no help! AA came out again and I had to get him to install a new battery. A very disappointing experience and I lost a whole day of my vacation! Plus if this had happened when my family and I were in the highlands of Scotland it could have had a totally different out come.

title: Baseless charges
text: Several days after returning a rental car, I received an email informing me that I would be charged $450 for a cleaning fee. The email listed potential reasons a cleaning fee could be applied, but said nothing about the actual condition of the vehicle I returned. I responded to the email for clarification, was told there was no charge showing on their end, and it must have been a mistake.   A few days later, there was a charge on my account, and I received a letter in the mail (it was identical to the email in its lack of explanation for the charge).   I called Budget to get more information, and was told that it was because the vehicle smelled like smoke and someone must have been smoking in the car. I do not smoke. Neither does my wife, nor my three young children. I told the agent that. She turned on a dime, saying that “oh, actually, it’s because of dust and dirt particles then”. It seemed clear to me that Budget just makes random charges hoping that people won’t bother disputing them. I told the agent I needed the charge refunded. She told me that she could give me a small discount “because the charge is valid”.   I called my credit card company to dispute the charge immediately.   Randomly accusing your customers of damaging your vehicles is bad enough, but not correcting the charge when it was questioned (and when the charge was, at best, made in error) is poor customer service and a bad business practice.

text: Even if you are on a Budget, Do not choose Budget!  I prepaid for a Budget rental on May 1, 2026, for the period of May 7–10.  On May 9 at approximately 6:00 PM, the vehicle broke down while my family and I were vacationing near Asheville, North Carolina. We were staying in a cabin in Hot Springs, about an hour away. I spent nearly three hours on the phone with Budget seeking assistance. I was initially told a replacement vehicle was available 15 minutes away, but after multiple transfers and lengthy holds, I was informed that the only option was an airport location approximately 2.5 hours away.  By this point it was around 9:00 PM, causing me to miss a reservation at Jargon, a highly regarded vegan restaurant. Budget expected me to take an Uber 2.5 hours away, pick up another vehicle, and drive 3.5 hours back to my cabin, requiring more than seven hours of travel late on a Saturday night before our scheduled checkout the following morning. Rather than lose the remainder of our vacation, I rented a vehicle from Enterprise, located only 15 minutes away.  After returning home, I contacted Budget. After receiving little assistance, I requested a manager, who initially offered only a one-day refund despite the vehicle failing during the second day of the rental. After considerable discussion, I reluctantly accepted.  A few days later, I discovered that instead of receiving a refund, I had been charged for an additional day. I called multiple times and was repeatedly told that Budget's records showed a refund had already been processed, despite my bank statement showing otherwise. Promises to escalate the matter resulted in no follow-up.  Around May 21, I called again and was told Budget's system was down. I called daily for a week and received the same response. Around May 28, I was again told I had already been refunded and was instructed to email proof from my bank statement. During that call, a manager became rude after I stated I did not wish to end the call until the issue was resolved and eventually hung up on me. Before the call ended, I informed him I intended to report the matter as fraudulent.  While speaking with Chase Bank, Budget finally reversed the erroneous extra charge, but the promised refund still had not been issued. Chase assured me they would assist through the dispute process if necessary.  On June 12, I contacted Budget again and was once more told they would issue only a one-day refund, despite the fact that I effectively had use of the vehicle for only one full day before it became inoperable. As of this writing, I am still waiting for the refund.  At this point, I believe a full refund is justified. The breakdown itself was frustrating, but the weeks of contradictory information, overcharges, lack of follow-up, repeated promises, and countless hours spent explaining the same situation have made this one of the worst customer service experiences I have ever encountered.  Even if you are on a budget, do not choose Budget.
address: 1000 Glenn Hearn Blvd SW Ste 108B, Huntsville, AL 35824, USA

text: Budget Car Rental Fairbanks:  Extremely disappointing and honestly unsafe experience.  We specifically reserved an all-wheel-drive SUV to handle snowy conditions in Alaska. What they did not tell us at pickup was that the vehicle had all-weather tires, not proper snow tires. Also I think one of the wheel slip sensors was having a problem. Every turn was slipping. Those differences mattered and we found out the hard way. We got stuck in the snow twice. The first time, a Good Samaritan helped dig and push us out. The second time, I had to call AAA for a tow truck to save us.  while stuck there serving time, I contacted the rental office and spoke with someone named Rita. While I was explaining the situation, she kept asking repeatedly if the car was damaged (it wasn’t), instead of addressing the actual problem. I mentioned the wheel slippage warning light flashing constantly, which didn’t feel safe at all. She told me to call back in five minutes so she could arrange help, but also made it clear we would have to pay for it. Fine, whatever, we just needed help; it was getting cold and dark. We called back in five minutes… and she had already left for the day. No follow-up, no support. WTH. She just did that so she wouldn’t have to deal with it. Can you believe that?  Thankfully, my own AAA came through and got us out. When I returned the car the next day, I told the staff that a mechanic should check the car because of the constant warning light. The response? Total indifference. No concern whatsoever.  Bottom line: this felt like a bait-and-switch on snow capability, put us in a genuinely unsafe situation, and came with poor customer service from start to finish. If you’re traveling in winter conditions, I strongly recommend using a different rental company for your safety and peace of mind. Zero stars.

text: I feel like I need to take up the mantle for this place for those of you who are in similar circumstances to mine. Loke was informative and efficient and we were provided essentially a brand new vehicle that was free of defects, blemishes or any evidence of previous wear and tear. Overall it was a spotless experience that I'm grateful to have gone through with. text: My 76 year old mom reserved a car from this location. Her flight to Phx was diverted to Las Vegas due to weather. She got in 4 hours later than expected. She was supposed to pick her car up by 5:00pm (when this location closes).  She called the location to tell them what was going on. The manager said if she couldn't make it by 5:00 she'd have to come the next day and that the price would increase by $100 (for one less day of the rental). When my mom said she didn't understand, that she would be there when they open the next morning and that what was happening was out of her control, the manager responded "Well, it's out of our control too".  My mom called back about 30 minutes later and was transferred to customer service. They said that the price should be the same as long as they don't close the reservation. They said if they close the reservation then the pricing will be based on the pricing for that day.  My 76 year old mom called the location as soon as they opened the next morning to ask. The location said that they could not tell her unless she came to the location, but that it was most likely she could get the same price as her reservation. What? They can't tell her for sure and she has to be there in person! So my kind, elderly mom gets an Uber to go to the location (since I couldn't driver her because I had to work that day).  When she arrives the manager tells her the price will be different, $100 more. Make that make sense. You have a reservation, you can pick your car up anytime prior to 5:00pm, your flight gets diverted due to weather, the place closes at 5:00pm and opens at 8:00am the next day. You show up at 8:00am the next day and they want to charge you a $100 more for a reservation you've had for over a month.  So, my 76 year old mom calls Enterprise, they quote her a price $6 more than her original price from this catastrophe of a business, and she takes the Uber to Enterprise and rents the car. For $94 less than the place she was trying to give her business to, but the staff was too dense to know how to run a business.  Be mindful before you decide to rent from this Budget. Know that there are other choices that actually provide service, and are reliable. Both my family and my wife's family live out of state, they will be renting cars out here for the next several years to come out and see their grandkids. I can assure you NONE of them will rent from this Budget, or probably any budget.

text: I have rented from Budget multiple times before, but I have to leave my honest review because this absolutely should not have happened. I understand that things do go wrong sometimes, but I can say with almost 100 percent certainty that Budget does not properly inspect their vehicles before transferring them to another customer.  I picked up a rental car from this location on 3/13/25, and within minutes of driving it, the tire pressure light came on, telling me to add air. I immediately took it to Discount Tire, where they told me the TPMS sensors in the tires were installed incorrectly—the readings were completely backward. One tire read 70 PSI, another 36, another 10, etc. The staff at Discount Tire managed to fill the tires properly and even mentioned that Budget should never have released a vehicle that wasn’t checked.  On top of that, this wasn’t even the car I reserved. I had requested a Toyota Corolla or a similar vehicle, but when I arrived, there were only two cars in the parking lot. One of them was a white Buick Encore, which they gave me. Since I was leaving for California that same day, I had no choice but to take it after they told me that neither this location nor any nearby Budget locations had any other available vehicles.  Halfway to California, yet another warning light came on—this time for the all-wheel drive system. Again, this was something that had clearly not been checked before they gave me the car.  Then the real nightmare began. The next day, while returning to our hotel, the car abruptly shut down in the middle of an intersection. Every warning light popped up on the dashboard, and the car was completely unresponsive—I couldn’t even turn it off. It wouldn’t move, and nothing worked. To make matters worse, we were nearly hit in the intersection due to the sudden system failure.  Thankfully, someone helped push the car into a dark, deserted McDonald’s parking lot. I had my two children with me, one of whom is autistic, and they were both terrified. We were stranded in the middle of nowhere at night, and when I called Budget’s customer service, I could barely understand the representative because he didn’t speak clear English. He just kept repeating that I “couldn’t abandon the vehicle” over and over. They then offered me an Uber voucher to go an hour away to the San Diego airport to pick up another car. There was no way I was going to leave my mother and my children alone in the dark to take an Uber with a stranger to a place I’d never been.  After pushing back, Budget finally agreed to send a tow truck and bring me another car. When the tow truck driver called me, he said the only car available was a Dodge Charger, so I had no choice but to take it. As if things couldn’t get worse, the tow truck itself broke down, and the driver had to wait for help before he could even bring us the car.  I was already unhappy with the Dodge Charger because it was not what I had originally reserved. I needed a fuel-efficient vehicle since we were driving from Arizona to California. So, we had to go to yet another Budget location to exchange it. Finally, we were able to get the car I originally requested—a Toyota Corolla. Even then, the navigation system barely worked with my phone.  Fast forward to this past Thursday when I returned the vehicle. To my surprise, Budget charged my card not once, but three times—for each of the vehicle swaps, which were due to their negligence. On top of that, they charged me a towing fee, which is absolutely insane. They also charged me for the half tank of gas in the car that broke down, even though it completely died and was undriveable.  In total, they charged my card nearly $1,800 when my original rental was only supposed to cost $474 for a few days.  I will NEVER rent from Budget again. I understand that things happen, but the way this entire situation was handled—the lack of accountability, the extreme inconvenience, and the absurd extra charges—is completely unacceptable.  Thanks, Budget, for ruining my entire vacation and ensuring that I will never rent from you again
address: 6650 E Superstition Springs Blvd, Mesa, AZ 85206, USA

text: We had a horrible experience with this rental location and the employee Freddy who worked there.  My wife and I had rented a car from the Mesa Airport location and were given an electric vehicle.  We do not have an electric vehicle and have never used one.  We did not want the vehicle, but the employee said it was a great vehicle and would be easy to charge.  Skeptical, we took the vehicle, a Subaru Solterra.  No details were provided on charging other than to download an app called Presto.  This app would show us charging location that we could charge at.  When we got into the vehicle it was not fully charged and a little dirty.  Driving the first day was fine and we found one charger while eating lunch in Gilbert, AZ.  We let it charge for 1 ½ hours and only received 30 additional miles.  We had other plans for the day and thought that we could find other chargers while on our vacation.  There were no other chargers close to any of the areas that we went to, and our hotel did not have one.  The next day we had planned to go to Scottsdale and drover there.  The Presto App showed multiple chargers in the area.  We went to 5 different chargers and each charger was broken or did not work with our vehicle.  Frustrated and having 40 miles left we called Budget Customer service.  We explained the issue we were having and told them that we wanted a different car.  We were told we could take the car back to any Budget location and exchange it.  There was a location in Scottsdale, so he headed there.  When we arrived, there was a sign on the door saying that the employee was out washing a car.  The sign stated that we could call, and the employee would be right over.  We called 3 times without any answer or call back.  Becoming more frustrated, we called Budget customer service again.  While discussing options with Budget customer service the employee returned to the location.  My wife asked the employee if he could help us.  The employee completely ignored her question and walked inside.  No apology for being away, no hello or welcome.  It was not until he was standing behind the counter that he asked how he could help.  My wife explained the issue and the employee, Freddy said “I don’t know if I have a car to exchange for you.”  Being frustrated with the entire issue and hours of time on our vacation being wasted, I told him that he needed to help fix the issue.  Without saying another word, Freddy told us “No, I’m not helping you and get the hell out of my store.”  Again, frustrated by the situation and now how we were being treated I told him we were not leaving until the issue was fixed.  Freddy continued to yell and tell us to get out of the store.  He then started to approach us in a very threatening way.  He approached us until we started walking to the door and continued to tell us to get out.  I do not recommend that anyone rent from this location or work with Freddy.  He is clearly not concerned about helping customers or providing any type of customer service.  Luckily, we had booked this car through Costco and called them.  They found us another location and helped us get the car fixed.

text: 1 star because 0 stars is not an option. Short version - avoid Budget car rental in general and definitely avoid this location. Long version  - Prepaid for a one way rental to San Diego, picking up Sunday at 9 am. That morning, as I'm driving to pick up the car, I received a call from the Budget pick up location and was told that they don't have any cars to rent. I sop the man on the phone from hanging up to ask what he can do to fix their mistake. After some back and forth he agrees to call other locations. While waiting I call the 800 number and try to get help from the reservation department. After a long search the representative tells me that the only location with one way cars available is the airport, which is 45 minutes in the opposite direction I need to travel! I declined his offer to change my reservation. By now I had arrived at the location and there were several people who were also stranded without cars. The counter guy is giving everyone the same story, "no cars available". While I'm there a woman returns her rental and I ask the counter guy if I can rent that car. He start to give me an excuse that maybe, but the car may need maintenance or be damaged. I stop him and ask if everything is fine with the car, then can I rent it? Now he tells me that there's one other person in that has a higher priority. This guy seemed like he just didn't want to rent any cars. On the way out another customer pointed out a row of about 10 cars that were rentals. At this point I chose to call and ask for customer service. The woman that "helps" me tells me that there's nothing that can be done and wants to cancel my reservation. I asked to speak to a supervisor and she starts talking over me telling me that they won't be able to help either. After I insist she puts me on hold. After 5 minutes she picks up the phone and tells me that their wait time is long for a supervisor. I choose to keep waiting and after 3 more minutes on hold they disconnect the call. I call the reservation line one more time and the representative is very sympathetic and looks for nearby Budget locations that show cars available. I got excited when she found one, only to be disappointed when she tells me the location is the place that I had my original reservation with! At this point I gave up and asked her to cancel my reservation.

text: I normally don’t leave reviews, but we were really disappointed with our recent experience at this location. After our flight was delayed several hours, we took the shuttle to the rental counter and found a long line with only one counter open. Once we finally reached the desk, checkout was quick — but when we went upstairs, there was yet another line to actually receive our car.  We were assigned a Tesla, and during my usual exterior walk-around I took photos of cosmetic damage. Unfortunately, I didn’t think to check the inside right away. The steering wheel was literally falling apart, and once we saw the car in daylight the next day, the interior was filthy — sticky residue on the glass roof and visible dirt throughout. Some interior paneling in the back seat was also falling off. I was honestly shocked that Budget considered this vehicle acceptable to rent out.  We called Budget and were told we could swap the car at any location, but when we went to a nearby branch, the agent said we had to return to the original airport. We went back the next morning and the swap itself was quick (no one even asked why we were changing cars), and the replacement vehicle was thankfully in much better condition.  However, when we returned the second car at the end of the trip, we were charged a fuel fee for returning the original Tesla at 64% instead of 70% — even though the only reason we brought it back early was because of its condition. We returned the second car at 85%. We’ll be disputing the additional charge.  Overall, this experience was frustrating from start to finish, and we expected much better.
address: 3355 Admiral Boland Way Ste R176, San Diego, CA 92101, USA

text: Notice the last 20 reviews , nearly all 1 star.  Beware of this place and be sure to videotape your car on return  ——I was charged a $125 fee for excessive interior cleaning in a  vehicle that on return had marginal amount of dirt. I have rented hundreds of cars and have never heard of this type of fee . We always rent Vans for our family of five on trips that require airfare and rental. We typically rent from enterprise and have  never seen this type of charge.  We we have used budget the last couple times due to enterprise being out of cars. Now, we see the reason why budget has more vehicles available as this has likely happened to other people. This is similar to the way in which Hertz used to be very popular until dishonest practices and now people rarely use hertz..  I have never been charged for a fee even when renting cars near a beach with excessive sand . There was no one to discuss this with, there was no one at the time of returning vehicle and no one contacted me other than sending a letter in a bill of 125$.  We flew to Fresno and this was an airport rental and did not have pet or have any excessive damage nor was there smoking in a family with  young children yet somehow a charge of $125 nearly 8 weeks later. In fact, there had not even been closure on the ticket two weeks later and  suspect this was some random charge on even the wrong vehicle given that no one had taken  account of the rental being returned until two weeks later. I had to contact budget when I noticed ticket had not been closed and they stated sometimes if busy the budget location has a delayed ticket closure. The ticket would not be closed for one week.  Is anyone aware of any type of class action lawsuit regarding this? If they randomly did this to every 3 to 4 customers they would rake in hundreds of thousands of dollars a year on false fees.  Now, I have learned my lesson, if we get stuck with a budget rental, we will have to videotape the car on return.  Do not use Avis budget at this location and consider at other locations .

text: I’ll preface this by saying I booked through Costco Travel, and while I know it’s best to book direct with the business you’re using, I’ve also never had issues prior to this with other rental agencies.  Booking the car was great no issues there, but as many others have stated, I did have about a 45 minute wait in the queue once I went to pick up the rental. Every other agency was dead empty of a line, except Budget/Avis, which had no less than a dozen people in line when I got there.  Once it was finally my turn, the agent who got me checked in was fantastic. I do wish I had gotten his name.  The rental wasn’t the issue, the 4Runner I got was perfect for my needs of transporting my grandmothers wheelchair and luggage. Drop off was simple and straight forward, even outside of their open hours of operation.  The problem is upon returning home I noticed my final invoice was $80 more  then my original booking, which I hadn’t ever had happen before. Usually I pay whatever Costco Travel says I’ll pay. Ok, fine. I wasn’t thrilled, but It is what it is. I wasn’t going to stress over it too much. Chalked the $80 up to some fees or taxes that weren’t disclosed. HOWEVER, 3 WEEKS LATER, I was charged an additional $250. No invoice emailed to me, nothing added to my e-receipt, no contact or explanation whatsoever ever.  So I called, and after talking with customer service I was informed it was a cleaning fee for “excessive sand in the back”. Say what? Firstly this is a rental in California, a vacation state…I could understand a fee if the car was covered in sand from a family of 5 tracking it all through the car.  I was the only occupant of the rental until the day I returned it. I went to the beach once, and when I left the beach I took my sandles off, banged the access sand off of then before I tossed them in the cargo area of the 4Runner.  That’s it. I wasn’t building sandcastles in the back. In the last 18 years of car rentals on our family vacations, when we had 3 kids going to the beach every day, using body boards and wet suit and dealing with the inevitable sand that gets in as a result, I have NEVER been charged a cleaning fee.  So my originally booked $285, 6 day car rental, closed out at $613.98.  To anyone else looking to rent a car from this Budget, I highly recommend you stop somewhere right before drop off and detail it yourself, lest you be charged excessive fees.
address: 1659 Airport Blvd, San Jose, CA 95110, USA

text: My experience with Budget Car Rental at Colorado Springs Airport was beyond horrific—it was an absolute disgrace. The customer service was an outright joke, and the lack of professionalism from the staff and the manager was jaw-dropping. They acted as if doing their basic duties was some personal favor, and that attitude only got worse as things progressed. I had requested to pick up the vehicle earlier, between 9-10 AM, only to be told it wouldn’t be ready until noon. The vehicle I received was nothing short of disgusting. An ID was left on the console between the driver and passenger seats, and there were wrappers from cookies and chips stuffed between the seats in the second and third rows. This car had clearly not been cleaned at all in between customers. At best, it looked like they did a lazy, quick vacuum, which didn’t even come close to properly cleaning the vehicle. I know this because I detail cars myself, and there’s no doubt in my mind that all they did was the bare minimum. I didn't feel comfortable addressing it because the manager was already incredibly abusive and unprofessional, and it was painfully obvious he knew the car wasn’t properly cleaned. Yet, somehow, I’m being charged for a full car detail. It’s outrageous! They tack on fees at their “own discretion” because of the lazy job they do. All that extra time didn’t include cleaning the damn car and then, instead of resolving the issue as any competent car rental company would, Budget and its staff decided to go on the offensive, tacking on bogus fees out of spite. Budget/Avis Employees at the Colorado Springs Airport location are careless and have a hostile attitude, this made it clear that they wouldn’t help resolve the issue, and if anything, I was genuinely concerned that they wouldn’t hesitate to leave me stranded—just like they did to the elderly couple. This was especially concerning as I had a family emergency to attend to and couldn’t afford to be stuck dealing with their incompetence. To add insult to injury, when I calmly explained that multiple employees had already provided false information, the manager refused to take responsibility. Instead of trying to understand the customer’s needs, he chose to be blatantly disrespectful to me and my wife, throwing the ID back at us and telling us to take our business elsewhere. His behavior was utterly disgusting, and it escalated to the point where he became loud and aggressively disrespectful to my wife. We had no choice but to record the interaction due to how unhinged he was. Like all typical hypocrites, the moment he realized he was being recorded, he immediately switched to playing the victim, acting as though he had been going above and beyond the entire time. It was a pathetic attempt to cover his own tracks, as he put on a show of false professionalism, trying to manipulate the situation while hoping to provoke me into a response that he could use against me. Fortunately, I remained composed, even though his behavior was a disgrace to Budget’s so-called “customer service” I wasn’t the only one treated like garbage at this location. The elderly couple was in their 60s or 70s being treated just as horribly, if not worse. They were told to get out of the line and walk away just because they were asking questions regarding their rental. The representative walked away to the back room and the elderly were left stranded, and the staff couldn’t have cared less. This location operates with a complete disregard for customer service and has zero accountability for their rude, retaliatory, and utterly disgraceful behavior. Do yourself a favor and avoid this place like the plague unless you want a dirty vehicle, abusive staff, and baseless extra charges. You'll end up paying extra just to clean a car yourself because the employees here do a terrible job. The only thing the manager and employees at this location seem to pride themselves on is rubbing it in your face that they don’t care about your business and telling you to take it elsewhere. ** No space to upload the video.
address: 7770 Milton E Proby Pkwy, Colorado Springs, CO 80916, USA

text: We rented a car in Denver and upgraded to a 4WD. They gave us a Dodge Ram pickup which I am fine with because I drive a pickup at home. However, this dodge ram didn't have a proper 4WD "high". It simply had 2WD and 4WD LOCK, (which is what you need to pull someone out of the mud but can't really be driven at higher speeds). But realistically, if you are renting a 4WD for a family vacation, it's going to be used on the highway at some point or another especially with vehicles that don't come with winter tires. A pickup truck in 2WD with summer tires is completely useless in the snow.  A couple days later the 4WD failed because the highway use burnt the clutch pack in the front differential and we needed to change the car. However roadside assistance refused to send us a car because we could still use technically move the truck in 2WD. They said the only thing we could do would be to drive to the closest Budget location to change vehicles. The closest location was an hour away but the person on the phone took the time to call (Eagle Airport) and called me back saying, they don't have a pickup but you could change to a Chevrolet suburban or Ford navigator. I decided to drive the hour to Eagle to change the vehicle because the upgrade was appreciated.  When we arrived at budget eagle airport, the woman working at the counter seemed annoyed that roadside assistance had made such promisses, she said she wasn't upgrading and she was giving me another dodge ram. I said no, we're going to have the same problem and it was clear they had plenty of trucks and a literal pile of keys on the board. I would not have wasted half a day of my vacation to fight with the person over vehicles she clearly had. She then she said she was giving us a small KIA at which I also refused because we were 4 plus ski gear and it wasn't going to work. Next she said she would give us a Ford Explorer but when I went to leave with it, discovered the vehicle smelled like cigarette smoke so I also rejected it fearing I would be billed the 450$ penalty that Budget fines it's users for smoking in their vehicles.  She again refused to upgrade despite the long line of beautiful vehicles outside that I saw as a tried that smelly ford explorer. When I came back in, she first said she could not switch the vehicle, claiming the system would not allow it (which I discovered later was a lie). Finally, she gave us a Buick that had damage all down the right side and within a day needed engine oil as a warning flashed on the dash. It also needed washer fluid.  In summary, there are 2 major problems here. First, the base model pickup trucks Budget is using is unfit for the task of passenger 4WD rental. And Second, the woman at the Eagle airport needed to remove her ego from the situation and simply maintain a basic level of company consistency considering the trouble the guest was having.  Thanks.
address: 217 Eldon Wilson Rd, Gypsum, CO 81637, USA

text: Review of Budget Center – Monday, October 7th  I arrived early at 8:30 AM for a truck reservation as I was moving from Colorado to New Mexico, and I had my dogs with me. The young lady working there told me that dogs weren’t allowed because the owner was allergic. No problem—I understood and could relate to that.  I mentioned I had a reservation for 9:00 AM, but she told me I’d have to wait until exactly 9:00 AM, or I’d be charged for an extra day. I agreed and waited. At 9:00 AM, I went inside and began the reservation process. I provided my driver’s license and the card that had been used to make the reservation.  She informed me she couldn’t accept the card because it was in Jessica’s name. I explained that Jessica had been with me but had to leave for work, and I couldn’t use my own card because it had been compromised in a theft. She continued to talk over me and remained firm, so I offered another card that I hadn’t planned to use, trying to follow their rules.  At that point, I showed my temporary paper driver’s license, as we had just moved back into the country. She said they couldn’t accept the temporary license. So, I went outside—while managing my leashed dogs—to retrieve my passport. When I returned, I placed the passport and card on the counter, but she told me I was being disrespectful and that I needed to hand the items to her directly. Frustrated, I slid the passport and card over the counter, which, admittedly, wasn’t the best approach on my part.  At that point, she refused to continue the transaction, citing my behavior as disrespectful. Let's recap: I complied with the no-dog policy, waited until the exact reservation time, followed their rules on payment and identification, and provided my passport when my license wasn’t accepted. However, I was refused service because I slid the documents over the counter in frustration, and I take responsibility for that.  After this experience, I decided to go to the U-Haul location just around the corner. There, I was greeted by Shayne P. and Madison, who were friendly and helpful. They accepted all my forms of payment, my driver's license, and got me into a truck right away.  I want to thank the Budget representative who I called on Tuesday, October 15th, to assist me after I was charged a no-show fee. She was very helpful and apologetic about the situation. She represented Budget with great customer service.  As for the young lady at the Budget Center, I sincerely hope she finds peace and send her nothing but love and compassion—because we all need it.
address: 1101 W Dartmouth Ave, Englewood, CO 80110, USA

text: Made reservations for a 5pm Connecticut pickup. They gave us a different size SUV even though we reserved a certain size months in advance. The vehicle was stolen overnight,  48 hours after of arriving at our vacation destination. We later found out that the vehicle they gave us has a very high theft rate. The Budget location in Montreal where it was stolen said they know it has a high theft rate and they refuse to carry it in their fleet. The Montreal location then forced another SUV on us even though we wanted a sedan due to another theft concern. We basically spent an entire day of our vacation dealing with the theft matter even though the theft was not due to our negligence. We were so traumatized by the event that we left our vacation early. When we returned the replacement vehicle to Connecticut the clerk was defending the decision to give us a different size SUV saying that I signed the contract. Now Budget wants to collect from my insurance. This will likely result in the loss of a no claim discount and higher rates. The clerk claimed the company would not know that they were giving us a high theft vehicle. Yeah right. They have data on everything. In my opinion, Budget was negligent and they are responsible for their own loss. I also want to add that Budget and likely other rental companies have zero modern anti theft protections. They have not kept up with the car theft criminals who use technology to steal vehicles such as hacking a car remote and driving away in the car in about a minute. Then we wonder why our insurance rates go up. The corporations should look at themselves for the reasons for losses and higher rates.
address: 58 Mill Plain Rd, Danbury, CT 06811, USA

text: When it came time to return our rent a car, my girlfriend did not want me going in the building with her because she knew that I would cause a stink and request a free day that we were charged extra for. That being said, I feel like leaving a critical review for a business in today’s Google-centric world is probably worse. Oh well….  When we had originally reserved a car from here, we had specifically requested an economy vehicle. When we went to go pick up the car, we were told that the only vehicle they had in a ECONOMY class vehicle was a Ford Mach EV. For those not familiar with that car, it’s a fully electric vehicle.  The car is absolutely fun to drive. Great torque and safety features. However, it is about as far from an economy vehicle as you could possibly get. To me, an economy vehicle is inexpensive to operate and gets great gas mileage. Unfortunately, we had to drive this car to Tallahassee, which proved to be extremely problematic. It gets about 190 miles on a full charge and a full charge takes about an hour to complete, and that’s if you are able to find a high-speed charger. As a comparison, my Volkswagen Taos gets about 400 miles to the tank full and takes about five minutes to fill up.  So, not only did we have to charge the stupid thing frequently, paying for the charging is actually twice as expensive as filling up a traditional gasoline powered vehicle. Additionally, because we had to charge the thing so often, we were late, bringing the car back and had to be charged an extra day. Not cool Budget!  I think there was a Seinfeld episode involving a rent a car company being great at taking reservations, but not being able to fulfill them. That was definitely the case here. Yes, we agreed to take the electric car. However, it was the ONLY car they had on the lot. So, they were great at taking our reservation and not great at fulfilling it. It smells a little like a bait and switch to me, but that’s up for debate.  Most people don’t have electric cars and are not familiar with what’s required to maintain them. Budget could’ve done a better job of educating us on what to expect with this particular vehicle. They could’ve been more understanding about the charging situation and comped the extra day they charged us. Electric cars are great for cruising around the city if you have a charger at home. They suck as a rental. Don’t let them fool you into taking a fully electric rent a car if you’re here on vacation or planning a road trip. You will be pissed off and disappointed.

text: Absolutely Horrifying Experience. Do NOT Rent from Budget (Tampa Airport)  This was hands down the worst rental car experience of my life and honestly one of the worst travel experiences I’ve ever had.  I was given not one, but TWO different rental cars that were infested with cockroaches from the Tampa Bay Airport Budget location.  Two days after picking up the first car, I found a cockroach inside. Customer service made me to drive all the way back to the original pickup location (even though I wasn’t nearby and asked to switch at a closer location). This alone took a full day out of my vacation. When I returned the car, they gave me a replacement — which turned out to be even more infested.  In the second car, a cockroach crawled across the center console screen while I was on my way to dinner. Thankfully I wasn’t actively driving at that exact moment because it easily could have caused an accident. When I got back into the car later, I lifted my backpack up off the passenger seat and at least three cockroaches ran out from under it. We checked the trunk and lifted the panel — there were many more.  There is absolutely no way this location is unaware of this problem. The chances of receiving two separate cars with infestations is not a coincidence. Budget is knowingly renting out unsafe, unsanitary vehicles with zero concern for customers.  The situation caused real financial and emotional damage: •	Cockroaches spread into my brother’s home (he has since found them and now needs pest treatment) •	I had to unpack my suitcase piece by piece outside and found five cockroaches in my luggage and had to pay to have everything cleaned before flying home •	I had to change my flight because I felt unsafe driving the car 45 minutes back to the airport •	I spent 10+ hours on the phone with customer service, roadside assistance, and being transferred back and forth with no real help  Customer service was a complete failure. I was told different things by different departments, offered only a $10 voucher, and later (after the trip was over) told I could have been reimbursed up to $50 for an Uber — information that was never clearly provided when it actually mattered.  My Christmas trip was ruined. I lost time with family who I do not have the opportunity to see often, dealing with infestations, cleaning, phone calls, and stress instead of enjoying my vacation.  I will never rent from Budget again under any circumstance. I am warning everyone I know — and everyone reading this — do not rent from them, especially from the Tampa Airport location. The conditions of those cars was absolutely disgusting, the thought of it makes my skin crawl. No one should have to go through what I did but I am sure that I can not be the only one, budget should do better and get their cars treated instead of unleashing this issue onto unsuspecting costumers.
address: 5409 Airport Service Rd, Tampa, FL 33607, USA

text: Unpleasant Experience with Budget Car Rental @TPA International Airport  My recent experience with Budget Car Rental at Tampa International Airport (TPA) was extremely frustrating and far from the level of service I expect as a frequent traveler.  👉To start, the pickup process was disorganized and concerning. I was charged twice for the security deposit—$250 each time—totaling $500. This immediately raised red flags about billing accuracy and internal processes.  👉Things only got worse when I attempted to locate my assigned vehicle. The first car listed for me was already occupied or assigned to someone else. I was then given a different vehicle, only to find out that it had also been double-booked for another customer. This level of confusion and lack of coordination is unacceptable and created unnecessary stress at the start of my trip.  👉Unfortunately, the issues didn’t end there. I returned the car on time—Monday, May 25 at 1:51 AM—with a full tank of gas, as required. Despite this, I received a text message three days later, on May 28, asking if I wanted to extend my rental. text: AVOID THIS COMPANY – PAY MORE ELSEWHERE AND SAVE YOUR SANITY If there’s one thing I’ve learned from this experience, it’s that Budget’s corporate structure is not designed to resolve customer disputes or stand behind their equipment. I typically rent from Enterprise, but because they don’t offer one-way box truck rentals, I chose Budget. That decision ended up costing me far more than I "saved." I reserved a 16ft truck and car trailer weeks in advance to move out of state. I made it clear to the staff that I had a strict 6 PM departure deadline because of a 14-hour drive, movers scheduled for 2 PM the next day, and my daughter’s 2nd birthday party that weekend. I expected a seamless process, but what I got instead was an exhausting and costly ordeal.  The Nightmare Timeline: Trailer Hookup Issue: After packing my entire home into the truck, I returned for the trailer hookup only to be told the truck wasn’t equipped with the proper towing control panel, meaning no functioning brakes or blinkers. The only “solutions” offered were to swap trucks (impossible after 4 hours of loading) or call roadside assistance.  Roadside Delays: Roadside arrived 4 hours later, fixed the wiring in 45 minutes, and I didn’t depart until 10 PM—already four hours late. Trailer Strap Failure: When I attempted to load my car, the trailer’s straps were rusted and jammed. Roadside had to come back with WD-40 and a hammer, delaying me another 2 hours. My family (wife, toddler, and dogs) waited anxiously so we could travel together for safety. We finally left at midnight. Blown Tire on the Trailer: Two hours into the trip, a trailer tire exploded due to bent fenders I was never informed about. We were stranded at a truck stop for over 5 hours while roadside sourced a rim and tire. My baby spent nearly 24 hours in a car seat, and we slept in the truck stop parking lot worried for our safety. The Financial Fallout: Lost $200 deposit for movers Paid an additional $600 for expedited movers Extra food and gas expenses Nearly $2,300 paid to Budget for equipment that was unsafe and poorly maintained  Customer Service Disaster: When I called Budget the next day, the local manager admitted he couldn’t do anything and told me to contact corporate. Corporate initially said they’d “comp the rental,” but then changed their position and only offered $200. After escalating, “Level 2” customer service offered $500 max—but only after I returned the truck, effectively leaving me no choice. It’s been nearly a month since then, and no one has followed up despite their promises.  Final Verdict: Budget is cheap for a reason. The money you save upfront will cost you far more in delays, stress, and out-of-pocket expenses. Their equipment is poorly maintained, their support is painfully slow, and their customer service is structured to deflect responsibility instead of fixing problems.  I have attached pictures just for visualization of all of the issues encountered. Do yourself a favor: rent from a company that values its customers and stands behind its service. I will never rent from Budget again—and if you value your time, money, and sanity, you shouldn’t either.
address: 975 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

text: I am submitting this statement to document my recent experience with this rental car location, which I consider both highly unprofessional and potentially deceptive in nature. At the branch, I was informed that I had been charged a $450 cleaning fee due to pet hair allegedly left from a prior renter. However, further discussion revealed that the inflated charges were not primarily due to this fee, but rather the result of daily insurance charges and undisclosed add-on fees that were never explained or agreed to at the time of signing the rental agreement.  The branch manager, Issac, was unable to provide clarity regarding my reservation, receipt, or a clear breakdown of charges. His demeanor during the discussion was combative and unhelpful, and he appeared uncertain about the company’s own policies and procedures.  Due to these unresolved concerns, I will be pursuing this matter with my bank, Cherokee County public services, and the Better Business Bureau. The billing practices I encountered appear misleading, and I believe they take advantage of customers in vulnerable situations through undisclosed fees and post-rental charges that materially deviate from the agreed terms.  I strongly urge corporate leadership to investigate this branch and its business practices. As a consumer, I have lost all confidence in this location, and I intend to take all appropriate actions to ensure accountability and prevent others from experiencing similar treatment.

text: I rented a car from this location for one day and unfortunately left my sunglasses on the dashboard when I returned the vehicle. The next day, I went back to inquire about them. During my conversation with the cleaning staff, I was told that the employee who originally cleaned the car was still off work and that communication with him was difficult. One employee even mentioned that valuable lost items are sometimes kept by that individual, which was concerning to hear. The front desk staff took my contact information again and assured me they would follow up after speaking with the employee who cleaned the vehicle. However, I never received a call or any update. While I understand that forgetting personal belongings in a rental vehicle is ultimately the customer's responsibility, I was disappointed by the lack of follow-up, ownership, and customer service throughout the process. text: The service at the desk is the worst compared to any other USA locations due to inefficient desk agent, coordination issues and poor direction. I understand this location operates inside a mall but when fast break members have advanced reservations, it is expected that the wait time would be minimal to zero. The option to pick keys (self service) or walk directly to car area should be available no matter what. At the very least, there needs to be a pick up brochure or an agent to help the members w/out line wait. The directions to the desk location were nowhere and there’s no signage inside the mall. Most shop vendors (on the mall first floor) also had no clue of budget desk location. Google Maps vaguely mentions first floor. To add to this incompetence, the agent at the desk was grossly slow and incompetent. People had to wait for over 20 minutes. Also, we were not able to find our car on the fifth floor as instructed and we had to go back down to wait another 20 mins to get another reassignment in the same vehicle class. Turns out, their work crew may have wrongly parked the car on 6th floor instead of 5th. The first agent at the desk didn’t know how to track this information whatsoever. Our itinerary had been badly affected due to the delays and inefficiencies. Avoid this location in future if possible and will definitely trust the recent google reviews.  Future tourists: The desk location towards the end of first floor escalators and is on the food court side (all the way near target). For drop off during off hours, enter through the parking garage entrance for the market place and head to the level 5. Make sure you return the 5 things (rental drop off date, drop time, mileage, fuel range and parking ticket) as in the attached screenshot. The drop box is located near the elevators.

text: I’m wondering how many customers have overpurchased fuel to cover the difference of their tank not being full when they pick up their rental vehicle?  I picked up my rental KIA vehicle from the Hilo Airport location at 7am in the morning. The mileage was 51833 miles and the fuel gauge indicated a full tank, as it should be when picking up a vehicle. After attending a funeral service and completing some short errands, I filled the tank at 11:20am at a nearby gas station. I filled the tank with 2.895 gallons of gas @ $5.699.  It seemed like a lot but I needed to fill the tank to full. When I returned the vehicle to the Budget Hilo airport at approximately 11:30am, the odometer read 51854.  I realized I only drove the rental car 21 miles. It didn't seem right that I refilled the tank with nearly 3 gallons of gas and only drove 21 miles. That would have meant that the KIA vehicle only got 7 miles per gallon?  At the airport I went up to the Budget counter person and said that I have a question about the refueling of the tank and that it does not seem correct. After briefly explaining the situation, she immediately raised both arms and said she was not the person to talk to, the male coworker next to her added that they don't know if I was sitting in the car for a long time with the engine on and using the fuel!  They never showed any interest and did not care about my inquiry. Adding insult to my situation, I felt they falsely accused me of fabricating my story. Having just attended a family funeral, this is the last thing I needed to end my trip to Hilo!  I've been a loyal Fastbreak customer using my corporate number for many years. I have always rented from Budget and have never been treated this way. I was treated very badly on this day and will not again rent from Budget Hilo airport, as there are other companies that surely want my business.

text: ⭐☆☆☆☆  This was honestly one of the most frustrating and disappointing rental car experiences we have had.  After getting off a seven-hour flight, we were exhausted and just wanted to get our luggage, pick up the car, and finally get settled. Instead, we spent about 15 minutes waiting in line just to receive the paperwork, and then another hour and a half waiting for the actual vehicle. After traveling all day, that kind of delay feels completely unacceptable.  The shuttle bus to the Budget location was very crowded and did not have much room, but considering how busy the airport was, that part was understandable and honestly not what upset us. The real issue was the rental car service itself.  When the car finally arrived, it was very disappointing. The vehicle had over 50,000 miles, multiple dents on the exterior, stains throughout the interior, and looked like it had not been maintained very well at all. They did wash the outside before bringing it to us, and the employee helping us was polite, which we appreciated, but it still did not change the condition of the car.  The car also did not come with a full tank of gas, which added even more frustration. On top of that, we had specifically requested a larger vehicle, but instead we were given a very small car with no real accommodation offered.  At that point, after such a long day of travel, it just felt defeating. We expected much better service and much better vehicle quality from a company like Budget. Instead, the experience left us exhausted, frustrated, and genuinely disappointed. Based on this experience, we would not recommend Budget as a company.

text: Exceptional Service – Jason Went Above and Beyond!  I can't say enough good things about the service I received from Jason at Budget. He truly went above and beyond to help me out of a tough spot.  I had never flown into Pullman before and tried to reserve a rental car online the day before my trip, but nothing was available. This seemed to simply be a glitch, however, as the message I received indicated that the rental starting time was after hours.  But according to Google, the rental counters would still be open when my flight landed, so I figured I’d try my luck in person. Unfortunately, both rental counters HAD actually closed by the time I arrived.  And with virtually no Ubers, taxis, or other transportation options in the area, I found myself completely stranded.  Desperate, I called the number posted at the counter, not expecting much. To my surprise, Jason answered (even though he had already gone home for the evening). After I explained the situation, he didn’t hesitate. He actually drove back to the airport to personally help me get a car.  Jason’s willingness to return after hours and help someone he didn’t know is the kind of customer service you rarely see anymore. What could have been a stressful and frustrating night turned into a reminder that there are still people who truly care. I’m incredibly grateful for his kindness and professionalism.

text: Long story short, I was visiting Yellowstone and had car problems and thought that budget was going to save the day. I reserved a car on Expedia and got a ride over there only to find that they would not rent to me because they require a credit card and will not accept a debit card or a deposit. They happily took the money for the reservation but the instant the receptionist saw that it was a debit card he acted like I was a homeless person asking to use his personal restroom. I have more than enough money in the bank to buy a brand new vehicle so for a receptionist to look down his nose because I don't believe in the usery of credit cards was pretty annoying especially after having already paid for the rental. . . . Luckily the very next kiosk over was Avis rentals and they not only happily rented to me using my debit card, but the clerk there was super friendly and put me into a 4x4 four-door Dodge ram 1500 which was amazing for going around Yellowstone. The best part was that it was $200 cheaper than budget was going to charge me for some little soccer mom station wagon. I drove the truck like it was my own and brought it back full of gas and in perfect shape. If you are reading this, and you are considering budget, I highly recommend that you at least reach out to Avis because in my experience it saved me money and actually prevented a lot of frustration. text: I am not likely to recommend renting from Budget to anyone, especially the poorly run Budget that I rented from on October 11th (3905 W 96th Street). I should have just refused the car, but we were leaving for Florida and didn’t really have time to waste trying to get a new one (or a clean one-if they had one). It honestly looked like someone just ate two handfuls of greasy food and then touched everything in there. (seats, console, steering wheel, dash buttons). There were crunched up cheese crackers still in the cupholders in the back seat and half eaten pieces of licorice in the seat pocket/under the seat. There was some sort of gel smeared on the inside glass of the driver's side windshield. There is absolutely no way anyone could say they "cleaned the car". I did take photos of the vehicle, ran it thought the car wash and then spent 45 minutes at my house cleaning the inside, along with vacuuming it, wiping the interior, and washing the windows.
