text: Echoing what others have said - my experience with the staff themselves at this location was absolutely stellar, and the rental process was great. I think the less-than-ideal stuff is probably coming from corporate, not the local staff.  I did just want to warn people - I rented a vehicle for a week, used it three times, never had any junk or animals or cigarettes in there at all. Still got hit with a $450 charge for extra cleaning, presumably off of residual pet hair from my clothing. Customers should be aware - to charge you the extra fee, rental companies have to have proof of *excessive* cleaning BEYOND standard. So if you get charged like I did, call corporate and ask for documentation/proof of excessive mess beyond standard wear and tear. (Be honest about it of course, dont abuse it, if pet hair is EVERYWHERE then thats a different thing. I know in my case it wasnt, at most it might have been a few hairs from my coat.)  I called corporate and had it handled and refunded easily, you just need to stay on top of it. This is true of any rental car company, which is why Im not blasting this local branch. Just be diligent, take your own photos, stay on top of extra charges, request itemization and documentation if it comes up.  Otherwise, the actual rental process here was perfectly smooth and the car was great, so no complaints with this specific local branch. (And again, very helpful staff!)
address: 912 S Walnut St, Bloomington, IN 47401, USA

text: They had the best prices compared to the other moving companies. I started comparing prices 3-4 weeks beforehand to see what they offered, check on promos, see if they offered unlimited miles, any discounts like AARP - which they did accept, & extra’s like a car dolly, if it came with a ramp, etc.  We picked it up on the date & time we reserved it for but it ended up being a 16 foot truck instead of a 12 foot, it did have a ramp which was extremely helpful, & also a back hitch if you needed one, we did not utilize. I didn’t really want one that big but it all worked out. You do need to provide your own lock on the back of the truck to secure your belongings & I believe you can purchase things like that right from the location you pick it up at, even moving blankets, boxes, bungee cords and A furniture dolly, etc.  It had a full tank of gas when we picked it up. It was one of there newer trucks & it got us almost 6 states away & through the mountains, snow, & even an ice storm! It took regular unleaded fuel & it had a 35 gallon tank. I maybe got 10 miles to the gallon, & it was way more than I figured it would be to fill it up every time gas prices are very high everywhere though right now and the cheapest fuel we found was 3.19 a gallon. I won’t say exactly what I paid because every location is different & this was a 1-way long distance move, it did Not have unlimited miles yet they offer about The amount you need & a tiny bit more is it, so be careful & is $1.00 for every single mile over what they give you unless you negotiate that ahead of time, which I did. There’s not a lot of room in the cab & my seatbelt was twisted & wasn’t right - I tried to fix it but couldn’t, it may have come like that from the factory, no one sat in passenger seat, or middle fold-down seat, the 1-cup holder held cups a bit odd & I lost a drink or 2 while going up a hill!🤣 but if that’s the worse thing to have happen I’ll take it! Good luck on your next move! Let’s just say we were happier than heck to bring the truck back! No offense, it worked great, just wore out after a move like that!
address: 814 W Coliseum Blvd, Fort Wayne, IN 46808, USA

text: We always rent from Avis/Budget in Noblesville and have always had great experiences... we rented a van for our vacation. I went to pick it up and they tell me that they are cleaning it and it'll be a few minutes, which was no problem. The lady with dark hair at the front desk was awful, rude, and simply should not be an employee. The girl that used to work there with lighter hair was always wonderful. I used to tell everyone to rent from Avis because we always had great experiences. I previously wrote a 5 star review. However, this time was the complete opposite..  After waiting 15 minutes for them to "clean" the car, the lady gave me the key and said, "it's right out there in the parking lot." I thought to myself that's weird, they usually walk around the vehicle with you to check for damage.. She then tells me if there's any damage to come back in and tell them. So, I walk out and there's a lot of damage, including the top of the hood completely bent up. I have never seen a rental car with damage like that before. So, I take pictures and walk back inside. I said, "did you see the hood on that van, it's completely bent up!" She then says in rude voice, "it's a rental car with 60,000 miles, it's going to have damage." She then tells me to mark it on the paper myself. Im pretty sure that's her job to mark the damage on the vehicle paper, but I went ahead and documented the damage on the paper myself. I walked back out and got into the van and started it. I was waiting for the it to warm up a little and I started looking around. This van was disgusting.. the floors were so dirty, there was trash in door and on the floors. That was just the few seconds that I looked around. We are about to take this car on vacation, so I went back in and told her it wasnt clean. She was so rude and said ill go clean it myself. She then got in the mini van and floored this van doing at least 35mph around the parking lot. About 10 minutes later, some other employee brings me the key. When I got back in the van, all they had done was pick the trash up in the door.. so at this point, I'm like they said they have cleaned this van twice, but clearly, they haven't cleaned it at all. After 45 minutes of me being here, I leave and decide I will just clean it myself..  So, after getting home I started to clean this van, which ended up taking us 2.5 hours. It was way worse that I thought. The van smelled like cigarettes and had burn holes in the carpet. The tires were dangerously bald. It had used BBQ cups and melted candy in the cup holders. It had slim jim wrappers, napkins, fake finger nails, sand, and gum stuck to the carpet and seats. The floors were filthy and had a lot of hair all over. There was crumbs and trash in the seats. I have never in my life seen a rental car this disgusting.  I ended up calling the 1-800 Budget customer service number to tell them about the situation as soon as i got home from leaving the rental place. That was really no help at all. The lady on the phone just said she would document the damage to van, like the hood. Which I'm glad she did, because it was ridiculous. Im assuming this is just how Avis/Budget operates now, because they didn't act like it was even an issue.  We returned the vehicle and cleaned it again before returning it, but of course the same lady didn't even bother checking the vehicle.  I will not be renting from Avis/Budget again after this. We ended up having to leave 3.5 hours later than we originally planned from our vacation, because of this rental. This company used to be great, but unfortunately they have really went downhill. I will no longer refer anyone else to this company. Please save your money and time and rent from a different company.

text: I walked in already carrying the weight of the world  and a dead car. As a single mom of three, when your vehicle gives up, it’s not just inconvenient… it’s a full-blown “Lord, is this a test?” moment. Then I met Sam. Now I’ll be honest  at first, I wasn’t sure. I was stressed, overwhelmed, trying to hold it together. When my card wouldn’t work for the rental, I just sat there for a second thinking, But then Sam came back out, saw the stress written all over my face and instead of brushing it off, he leaned in to help. He told me about options through Veridian Credit Union and gently guided me through the process. When I tell you this man was heaven sent, I mean capital-H, choir-singing, sun-beaming-through-the-clouds heaven sent. He didn’t have to care. He didn’t have to take extra time. He didn’t have to make sure I understood everything or reassure me that it would work out. But he chose to. And that choice meant everything. Some people might say they’ve had different experiences, but sometimes personalities just miss each other in translation. What I experienced was someone who genuinely wanted the best for me. Sam wasn’t just doing a job   he was being a human being. A really good one. He got me into a safe, reliable car that fits me and my three babies comfortably which is no small task  we travel like a tiny parade. He communicated clearly, responded quickly, and made sure I felt secure every step of the way. I didn’t feel judged. I didn’t feel rushed. I felt supported. For the first time that day, I could breathe. I walked in stressed, defeated, and unsure. I walked out relieved, grateful, and driving a car that works  which, let me tell you, feels like winning the lottery when you’ve been stranded. By far the best experience I’ve ever had. Sam wasn’t just helpful  he was a guiding hand during a moment I really needed one. And I’ll never forget that. Definitely recommend
address: 637 S Ankeny Blvd, Ankeny, IA 50023, USA

title: On of the Greatest Smart Mobility Solutions
text: Zipcar has consistently been one of the most practical and innovative mobility solutions available. The ability to reserve a vehicle by the hour or day through a simple app makes transportation flexible, affordable, and convenient without the costs and responsibilities of car ownership.  The vehicles are generally clean, well-maintained, and located in convenient areas throughout the city. For professionals, students, travelers, and occasional drivers, Zipcar provides an excellent balance of accessibility, reliability, and value. The reservation process is straightforward, pickup and return are seamless, and the variety of vehicle options helps meet different transportation needs.  As urban transportation continues to evolve, Zipcar remains a leading example of smart, sustainable, and user-friendly mobility. Highly recommended for anyone seeking convenient on-demand transportation.

title: Buyer Beware!
text: My wife and I were headed upstate to an Airbnb and rented a Zipcar to get up there as we do now own a car. As Brooklyn residents we’ve used their service for years. They’ve recently shifted from providing you with a physical key to having a key fob locked and hidden somewhere within the vehicle. When attempting to start the car again on our journey the key fob battery was dead and the car would no longer start. I figured that’s a $5 fix for a new battery.   I contacted customer support and was told they could not disclose where the key is located in the car for “security purposes” and would have to arrange for the vehicle to be towed. However rather than being able to use the tow company a mile down the road, they could only use their authorized tow company which would take a few days to pick up the vehicle. They essentially left my wife and I stranded. What could had been a $5 battery replacement turned into multiple hundred of dollars to tow the vehicle. I do not understand why Zipcar will gladly abandon their vehicles and their customers for days on end.

title: They really don’t value customer loyalty
text: I’ve been a customer of this service for a few years now. At first, things were okay, but over time, I ran into a lot of issues. They took unauthorized funds from my account more than once due to mileage miscalculations and took days to fix it without offering any meaningful compensation.  When I moved to Dallas, it got even more frustrating. I’d arrive at a location only to find out the car wasn’t available. The whole point of this service is supposed to be renting a car on demand, and that’s the last thing I’ve experienced. Instead of getting a quick, convenient rental, I’ve had to deal with constant miscommunications and last-minute changes.  On top of that, there’s a real lack of membership loyalty. They’re quick to collect monthly fees, but when it comes to offering real solutions or compensation, they fall short. Most of the time, I had to be the one to suggest canceling because they wouldn’t offer any real resolution.  In short, this service could be so much better if they actually focused on the core promise of on-demand rentals and valued their loyal customers. Right now, it just doesn’t feel like they do.

title: Unreliable
text: I’ve been using Zipcar on and off for about two years, and unfortunately my experiences have been unreliable. On multiple occasions, Zipcar has taken money that didn’t belong to them and, while they did eventually refund it, the process always came with poor customer service.  The first issue happened when I booked a 7-hour trip (11 p.m. to 7 a.m.), but their system changed my reservation from a 6-hour booking down to only 1 hour. I was still charged the 6-hour price for that shortened trip, and then they tried to charge me again for the original 7-hour reservation. They actually took the money from my account before eventually refunding it.  The second issue came when they incorrectly charged me $128, claiming I didn’t return the car to the right location. In reality, I returned it on time and to the correct spot. They did refund the money, but not without putting a hold on my account. After the hold was lifted, they then deleted my account, saying I hadn’t paid the membership fee — even though I couldn’t pay it because of the hold.  Overall, while Zipcar did eventually return my money, the repeated billing errors, account issues, and poor customer service make the service unreliable.

title: Poor Communication and Unfair Treatment
text: I want to leave a clear record of my experience with Zipcar. After canceling my membership years ago (and even receiving a refund at the time), Zipcar continued to charge me annual fees without providing any service. When I contacted support, the communication was slow, inconsistent, and dismissive.  At first, Zipcar admitted the mistake and refunded one charge, but then refused to refund earlier charges even though I provided proof of my cancellation and refund history. The company’s explanations contradicted themselves — they refunded my membership in one year while claiming my account was still “active” in another. This contradiction was never explained, and my concerns were brushed off as “policy.”  In the end, they marked my case as “resolved” without ever fairly addressing the real issue. This kind of communication and refusal to take responsibility is unacceptable for a company holding people’s payment information.  If you’re thinking about using Zipcar, be very careful — keep written records, check your statements, and do not assume “cancellation” means what it should. My experience shows how poor communication and a lack of accountability can lead to years of unfair charges.

title: Dirty Cars, Dirty Tactics
text: I am a college student and having ZipCar(s) near my campus seemed like a great thing for me to use, pretty affordable and easy access in the case that I needed to drive anywhere. Unfortunately I have come to figure out that the cars are 1. Not well maintained, and 2. Not clean. I rented a simple Kia Forte for an hour and a half to run an errand, I was met with a car with visual damage that seemed a little off putting. The damage itself wasn’t enough for me to reject the car, as it was mostly cosmetic (cracks in front and rear bumpers, tennis ball sized dents on multiple locations) and so I continued with the inspection process. When I opened the car though, the smell was horrible. I thought ZipCars were supposed to be non smoking vehicles? I was met with the distinct and very recognizable smell of a car that has had a smoke smell MARINATE. It’s not a recent event in that car’s life. The car itself was also dirty on the inside, crumbs lining the floor, coins scattered in the central storage, etc. I made SURE to note all these defects in my pre-trip inspection. Unfortunately for me I couldn’t cancel and rebook as this was the only car available at the time and my errand was urgent and couldn’t wait. I had to drive with the windows down in 60 degree weather to make the smell manageable. At the end of my rental I attempt to contact the text support but am frankly left unsatisfied, so I revisit it in the morning. Support not only proceeds to have extensive waiting times for responses but also just ghosts me for around 6 hours until I’m fed up enough to call in person. While seething in the fact that support left me unanswered for so long I check my bank account, now tell me why, as a broke college student I see not one, not two, but THREE separate charges for the same amount on my card? (Thankfully one of them seemed to have been removed) Now I sure as heck do not have movement like that and can overlook an extra 30-40 bucks missing from my account. So this is when I call support. The lady in which I speak with is dejected and frankly unhelpful. When I mentioned my previous chats with text support I audibly heard her SIGH as if I’m wasting her time and day trying to solve the issue?  I was nothing but kind and respectful as I know tech support is an exhausting job to deal with but that doesn’t mean I’m going to take it kindly when you seem unhappy to DO YOUR JOB. Hopefully this app can fix itself and its ways, my money better be returned in my account in the “3-5 business days” as stated otherwise there will be a big issue, as well as the “30 minute credit” they promised as a result of the frankly nasty car. I hope others have a more positive and pleasant experience with this rental app than I have had but I think I’ll stick to walking, public transport, or other means of travel.

title: SCAM
text: I rented a car last week and went to park in the public parking spot in my neighborhood, only to find it was blocked by another Zipcar and I had nowhere to park. I spent over an hour driving around looking for parking spots (on the phone with Zipcar support the whole time) where they recommended I park in a lot 1-4 miles away and 'walk home' despite renting the car amidst a busy day and nursing an injury. They promised repeatedly they were filing this incident on my account and I would not be charged if the car was towed or received parking tickets, only to be charged $100+ to both extend the car (which the employee lied and said was free since I spent the hour looking for parking and at a huge inconvenience to em) as well as for the parking ticket. I'm grateful I knew better than to leave it parked in a tow away zone because these scammers would've charged me $1k+ for that issue. After 5 calls, 3 emails they are now saying they don't have any record of the issue on my file (despite multiple employees seeing it before) and demanding I share evidence (which I don't have because it's a phone call). I just cancelled my membership and reported the fraud to my bank and the better business bureau. Do not use this company - they will charge you exorbitantly for things ttheir employees tell you to do, while refusing to provide any credit for the inconvenience and time lost (both trying to park and 5x calls later).

title: The app is not working correctly.
text: It's too expensive. Customer support isn't very good. If a user has problems with the car or the app, you'll have to wait a long time for help from customer support. The last time I called the support call center, I waited for 40 minutes. I couldn’t start the car because the app was glitching. Meanwhile, the rental time had already started, and I could only wait for a response from support. After 40 minutes, they answered the call and gave me another car from the same parking lot, adjusting the rental time to account for the delay. However, they didn’t refund me for the 40 minutes I had already spent waiting for support, and I was still charged for that time. Essentially, they just moved the rental period later, as if I had started the rental later, but I still paid for those 40 minutes of waiting. Later, when I tried to return the car on time, the app glitched again and wouldn’t let me end the rental, even though I arrived at the return location on time. The app kept tracking time and charged me for the extra minutes while I was trying to contact support again. Despite returning the car on time, they never refunded me for the extra charges for the time I couldn’t end the rental due to the app issues. It seems like this company is irresponsible toward its customers. They should follow basic service standards, like those in European car-sharing services. Despite the highest rental prices, this service is the most frustrating. Every time I used it, something went wrong.

Save yourself the headache and use literally ANY other car-sharing service. I've never seen such a backwards, user-hostile system in my life. Deleting this app and never looking back. Absolutely pathetic service in 2025."

title: Only use as a last resort; even then check ALL other options first.
text: The below is from a recent trip end report which is just one among many annoyances using this service. I’ve been using this service on and off for almost two years and it’s always been a pain.  Often cars are dirty and smelly also. And more than once l've had to refill nearly flat tires. In between personal cars I have to use it for longer errands but as soon as I have another option best believe this will be cancelled. Also you have to pay the $25 application fee anytime you cancel and reactivate and no options for pausing. Total ripoff and scam!!  When checking out the car, I had to do the full inspection 4-5 times because the app would not let me submit. Finally I started going through and choosing the boxes that would let me submit. Only certain combinations of checked box and text in box would let me submit. In the end in fact I don’t think I was even able to leave a text comment. Overall, if I didn’t need this service (and I will unsubscribe as soon as I don’t) I wouldn’t be using it at all. The experience itself plus customer service has been awful. I recently had to carry $1000 of damages on a work trip because the support team would not answer my questions or help out at all even though it was a hit and run at the location the zipcar was always parked. Absolute nightmare. If anything I’ll actively tell people NOT to use zipcar unless their life depended on it.

title: Terrible support
text: So many times your car doesn’t star, it’s out of gas, has a maintenance issue, been repeatedly hotboxed and trashed, leaves you abandoned somewhere, someone books right after you when you realize you need five more minutes, the gas card is missing or doesnt work and you have to go through a slow reimbursement process.   You have to fill up on gas on your own time. You have to fill up tires on your own time. End up being late for any of the issues above you get fined 50$ even though it’s a subscription service and youre paying the insurance and the cars are all booked when you need them.   Customer service can help solve your problems when you get to them but they never give you credits or refunds.   The system itself works and has helped me a lot but ive had several really bad experiences that i received no compensation or consideration for that has prompted me to leave this review.

title: Crapshoot Capitalism
text: If you book, know that your car may not be there. When you get there, only to find it’s not, the response of this corporation is to refund what you paid and give you a credit for an additional hour, to be used within the next 60 days. But that one-hour credit doesn’t always reflect the loss one suffers financially from not having access to the transportation they paid for.    Why allow a customer to book a car the company knows is not available?   Having used the service a lot, it’s clear this is a business practice. The loss you suffer as a result is not one they’re willing to compensate quickly or easily.   Get a Lyft or a cab if you need reliability.

title: Tire exploded, 3 hours for a false rescue, and a customer service that keeps hanging up on customers
text: Used Zipcar since 2019 and initially it was fine. Recent years I’m getting more ill-conditioned cars and the customer service has down-sloped.  Booked a car a week age. Yesterday was told my car was “no longer available “ for a mysterious reason. Got a replacement car miles away and Uber there.  Got a flat on the highway and their car didn’t carry the tools to fix it. Called CS and was assigned a service request.   They never came, leaving us waiting on the phone and biting dust for 3 hours. Finally called police and got a rescue. The police measured and said the tire pressure was 20 PSI, instead of a standard 60.   Returned the car with a replaced tire to the parking. Arrived home late night. Called Zipcar to try and get covered by the extra charge from the rescue.  TBH 85$ wasn’t much. Just wanna let them know the issue and make sure nobody would get hurt again.   Guess what? The same Indian lady, after hearing my purpose, hung up on me 3 times in a row.

text: I live in the middle of Detroit without a car. I tried out zip car at Wayne State for several months. I would recommend the service if they held up their end of the agreement. Dealing with all of the unknown conditions before you start driving can eat into your rental time and make you late if you have to be somewhere at a specific time.  The key situation is always different. The Bluetooth can have a hard time pairing. They only had small cars available. They increased the hourly rate soon after I became a member. It’s cheaper, with more car options, and better service to go through a rental agency. I don’t recommend. It’s always a hassle.

title: They waste your time and then call you late.
text: Even if you return the car before its return time, the app then asks you to take several photos, which is difficult and time consuming in the tight parking spaces you’ll find these cars in; once you’re done, the app will say “you were only a few minutes late” and then say they’ll close your account if they see a pattern for rule breaking. Terrible—the app is what wastes time in the first place. Most likely the account closure for “lateness” would be automatic for a tech oriented company like this, so your only option is to return the car early, with an open ended buffer based on whatever return process is implemented in the app, which could fluctuate unexpectedly in the amount of time it takes. The return time should be based on when I tap the “end trip” button, with the lengthy return process not counting toward that, unless I stop interacting with the app and it times out or something, which should then require me to tap the “end trip” button again. It’s common sense from a UX perspective.

title: Going wrong fast
text: Zipcar has been messing around with its model for the last year to maximize profits. I live in a major city and understand that this is a typical thing for a company to do, but imagine my surprise when I logged in today to realize that the price of every single car in my area has gone up by over $3 for an hourly rate within the past month. This means you are paying an extra $10 than you would have a month ago to rent a car for 3 hours. $20+ for day time rentals. The system used to be a reasonable and ecofriendly alternative to convenient travel, but is now honestly more foolish to invest in rather than just leasing a car or taking an Uber. It’s MORE expensive than just renting a car from a local car rental service. Let that sink in. Something that uses ten times less staff, doesn’t have to pay rent for a physical location, etc, is MORE expensive for HALF the expenses.  Zipcar when it was affordable was great!! I’d reccommend it to anyone! Zipcar now is overpriced and mediocre, and unreasonable to pay for a car on top of your already high monthly subscription fee. Bye Zipcar.

text: I’ve had an account with zipcar for years now, almost a decade and I swear it just gets worse and more expensive every time I open this app. I never rent a car for leisure. Call if there’s anymore problems.” Next, gas card gets declined 4x at one gas station and 3x at another but we’re fined if we don’t return car with gas at a certain amount. So again, I have to call. He’s moving slow, talking slow and I just WANT TO GOOOOO!! “Send me pictures of the final inspection.” FOR WHAT? I was never able to do a starting inspection!  You can never talk to someone who speaks English. They can always “barely” hear you. The prices go up dollars when you reach Friday and literally, they gave up on maintaining these cars and keeping them clean or at the very minimum, SANITARY! Hold these drivers accountable! There’s pet hair in a car while I’m going to pick up someone with a pet allergy! Like be for real! All the money you all want and the service is laughable. It’s definitely time to invest in a car because I’m starting to become extremely angry with this service….well, the lack thereof.

title: Beware of price gouging and poor customer service
text: Joined because I needed a car for Friday night but didn’t want to pay to rent for a whole weekend with a major rental company for $220+tax - I read up on zip car and saw that I could rent for a day for $103+tax and I didn’t care about the size of the vehicle.  Promo for first month free so there was no membership fee but was forced to pay $27.20 for membership application “processing fee”. Once approved I looked for cars in my area.  The cheapest car was a Kia or Chevy Malibu for $225+tax for one day!! This was a 120% markup for a non-holiday weekend.  Definitely reeked of price gouging and tbh - if I were to pay $250 for a rental I’d rather get a car for the whole weekend for rhe same price and get a much nicer Toyota or Nissan.  I called customer service to cancel and they wouldn’t refund my application fee (Josiah) and I demanded to speak to a supervisor.  They tried transferring me to a second customer service rep pretending to be a supervisor but learned he wasn’t a supervisor. Waited 30 mins for a supervisor and they disconnected me.  Called back and another cust rep (Jean) agreed to refund the membership fee and said she never heard of such an increase for a weekend in all her years working for them.

title: For Uber users
text: Customer service is horrible. App barely works. Can’t even sign in because it wants to verify my identity but NEVER sends me the code. I’ve tried deleting and redownloading but something is wrong with their systems. Every time it asks to send me a code to verify the code is never sent.

title: Unreliable cars and jaded customer support
text: I’ve been a Zipcar member for over 15 years, and lately there has been a massive uptick in the amount of members failing to return cars on time.  I’m not talking about 5-10min late, I’m talking about HOURS.   In the 15 years I’ve been a member, I had never once experienced this once, and then suddenly in the last few months it’s happened 3-4x with the SAME location.  Zipcar used to drop members who did this, but clearly that’s not happening.  They need to increase the late fee to $500 and drop members who do it more than 2x in a 12mo period–that would probably eliminate the problem altogether.    Worse, when you call Zipcar customer support, they are super blasé about it.   I feel like that $50 late fee should be given to the member it impacted.  But customer support doesn’t seem to care.  They offer to try to find you another car nearby, often one of lesser quality and multiple blocks away—which is a problem for me as a disabled person.  Then, they proceed to charge you the SAME fee as if you got your original rental—not discounting it at all.  Even better, you actually have to ASK them to change the reservation to remove the period you paid for that the car wasn’t available for.  Today was the final straw and I cancelled my membership.  Zipcar used to be great, but it’s terrible now.

title: Security privacy issues

title: Horrible
text: I am incredibly disappointed with Zipcar's customer service and policies. Recently, I was charged a $50 late fee despite being in the parking lot with the vehicle, simply because I hadn't clicked the "return car" button on the app. The reason for this delay? I was thoroughly cleaning the car, far better than how I received it, in an attempt to set a higher standard of cleanliness for other users.  As someone who always strives to be a good customer and do the right thing, this treatment feels like a punishment. I was in the parking lot, with no customers waiting, ensuring the car was spotless. Yet, Zipcar showed no understanding or flexibility regarding my situation.  Charging a $50 late fee under these circumstances is absolutely ridiculous and shows a lack of appreciation for customers who go the extra mile. Zipcar, this is not how you retain loyal customers. I expected better understanding and support, but instead, I am left feeling frustrated and undervalued.

title: Nightmarish Zipcar Experience Portland
text: Buyer beware!  I have been using zip car since 2021 in Portland, OR.My rental experience has progressively gotten worse over time until now, in May of 2024, when I am seriously considering unsubscribing from them. I tend to rent a car from them at least twice a month.  The following factors are present in about 75% of the cars I rent from them:  1. Cleanliness: Pet hair on seats, floor, or trunk Cigarette burns on seats Stains on seats Sand on seats Sand in trunk Trash on seats Stains on center console  Trash left in trunk Trash left under the seats Trash left in door storage areas  Typically several of these cleanliness factors are at play. I rarely encounter a zip car that is relatively clean.  There is zero accountability for the filth and dirtiness that these customers tend to leave behind.  2. Issues with app: App routinely fails to properly communicate with car locks so you are unable to end the trip because the app will not lock the doors. App must be restarted because bluetooth lock command fails to communicate with the car  3. Issues with customer support Routinely deal with 20-40 minute wait times when issues with the car occur. Time of day doesn’t matter nor does priority customer route make a difference. If you have an issue with your current car and need another one, customer support reps tend not to compensate you for lost time with more time on your active trip; rather, they give you a credit for the next trip.  If you shorten your trip by accident and then end it, they will not refund you some of the trip.    4. Issues with parking spots Zip car parking spots are poorly marked with small signage. People often trespass by parking their personal vehicles in your reserved zip car spots, forcing you to park elsewhere and notify zip car of your new parking location. You are not compensated for this time wasted.  5. Issues with the cars: Cars will have issues with tires, external damage, warning lights on dashboard, etc (caused by previous customers) which I have reported in the past repeatedly with the same cars. Zipcar does not repair them nor take them out of service after my reports. They continued to rent them out despite having some of these issues. They appear to care more about the bottom line than the safety of their customers  In conclusion, the zipcars in Portland tend to be filthy and disgusting and will typically have some external or internal damage.  If that isn’t an issue, the app typically will malfunction by failing to communicate with the locks on the car.  Additionally, customer service will invariably try to cheat you out of your money by giving you a credit on future trips.  This service in it’s current state is nearly unusable.  I suggest you stay away from it.

title: Terrible app

title: Uber Rental - Too Expensive
text: I rented a car for about a week and it was too expensive. The rates change per day and Zip Car doesn't offer weekly rates. This may be a good option if someone wanted to drive for a few days. Everything is remote and no local office or help. You have to call to extend for a day or more instead of using the app. The car needed to be detailed and missing parts such as the cap for the charge plug. It is a newer model electric car. Zip Car pays for the charging with EVGo but you can't get any help from EVGo if you have an issue at the charging station. I tried and I couldn’t verify their account. Customer support is outsourced and they don't know much or help with anything. One day the car wouldnt start. I’m going to rent a car through Avis for less than what I paid ZipCar. Renting should only be temporary because the companies are the only ones that profit.

title: Cars often need repairs, maintenance, and smell like smoke
text: We have tried Zipcar a three times now and have yet to have a good experience.   The first time our car was in a gated lot and we were not able to get out of the lot. After an hour of wasted time and talking to customer support they realized the building’s management had changed the gating system and they forgot to update their zipcars.  The next time we took a Zipcar a bit farther out to go for a hike and realized the car had no key, and we could only get into the car using our cell phone. We were concerned at the trailhead we might not have service and called support, and they confirmed we couldn’t get in without cell service and suggested we park somewhere else… what a useless suggestion. I’m not comfortable taking a car that doesn’t come with a key, if your phone dies or you don’t have service you are screwed.  Most recently we took a car again and upon picking it up it reeked of cigarette smoke. We didn’t want to cancel our plans so reported it in the pre trip inspection and went anyways. 5 minutes into the trip we got a warning that the rear tire was at 15 PSI, so we had to go well out of our way (not trivial in NYC) to get air for the tire. Zipcar wouldn’t refund us for any time because we didn’t call them during the issue and request a new car. Given that’s their policy I think we are done with Zipcar, it’s not reliable enough to really plan for anything because if there is an issue they want you to call them and wait around for them to try and find you some other car you need to walk blocks over to. We just paid 150 bucks to take a stinky car for 6 hours and spend 30 minutes of that time finding a gas station in NYC to pump up the tire, or cancel our plans for the day. We could just Uber for less money and fewer problems…

title: The worst customer service I’ve ever experienced
text: Their cars are cheaper to rent than most traditional services. However, it is not worth it. The price you pay in time wasted with cars that you booked that are subsequently unavailable and then dealing with customer service agents who are unable to help resolve the situation in any way far outweighs the benefit of their lower costs. The only reason I was a member for so long is because every time they would screw me over the only thing I was offered was service credits. Then trying to use those service credits, I would get screwed over again, and the cycle repeated. Never again.

title: Sometimes good sometimes $#!tty
text: I’ve had decent experiences before with Zipcar customer service, but more often than not in frustrated by the lack of understanding by reps. I explicitly started my most recent call prefacing that my account has locked me out due to password error (months since i last used the service but needed an affordable car rental, so i put my pride to the side) I also mentioned that I had proceeded my application fee for the wrong and could he help me? Not only did he tell me he couldn’t help me process the payment, he also told me too just reset my password, after i insisted that I just did that And got a message to contact customer service. After being made to explain the situation 2 more times, all ofc the sudden he can help me process my payment. Being frustrated i asked to speak to a supervisor, to which i was told they were busy and would call me back. This was Thursday of last week and I’ve yet to hear. title: Don’t waste your time and money

title: 50% reliable
text: Half the time, my car is unusable.  It could be because the car wasn’t at the location stated on the map (it was 2 miles away) or because somehow through a technical glitch the car is “checked out by another member”.  In any case, if you ever decide to use Zipcar, prepare for 45 minutes on hold with their customer support.  I end up needing to call for over half of the trips I take with Zipcar, and they’re always “experiencing elevated call volume.”  This company doesn’t care about self service or great customer support, and I guess it must not be good economics.  Anyways, give yourself an extra hour or two to deal with Zipcar.  Definitely 2 stars.
